markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 259 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Anubis](/Deities.aspx?ID=511), [Yamatsumi](/Deities.aspx?ID=319), [Zipacna](/Deities.aspx?ID=604) **Range** 30 feet **Area** a horizontal earthen or sandy surface within a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) **Defense** Reflex **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You cause the surface to heave. The area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586), and any tracks in the area are destroyed by the churning ground. Creatures standing in the area take a –1 status penalty to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). The first time each round you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the Spell, you can move the churning area up to 20 feet in any direction. Creatures immobilized by the spell are carried along with the shifting sand in the same direction, if possible. Creatures that enter or begin their turn standing in the shifting sand must attempt a Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and ignores the area’s difficult terrain and penalty to skill checks until the end of its turn.
**Success** The creature ignores the area’s penalty to skill checks until the end of its turn.
**Failure** The creature is affected normally by the spell this turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) within the spell’s area until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). If the creature was already immobilized by shifting sand, it also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). --- **Heightened (5th)** The status penalty increases to –2, and the spell’s range increases to 60 feet. **Heightened (7th)** The status penalty increases to –3, the spell’s range increases to 60 feet, and the spell’s area increases to a 30-foot burst. **Heightened (9th)** The status penalty increases to –4, the spell’s range increases to 60 feet, and the spell’s area increases to a 40-foot burst
name: Shifting Sand

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 260 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Ayrzul](/Deities.aspx?ID=687), [Mhar](/Deities.aspx?ID=571), [Mugura and Nrithu](/Deities.aspx?ID=300), [Orgesh](/Deities.aspx?ID=576), [Ragdya](/Deities.aspx?ID=618), [Yamatsumi](/Deities.aspx?ID=319), [Zipacna](/Deities.aspx?ID=604) **Area** 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) **Defense** Reflex --- You create a wave of energy that ripples through the earth. Terrestrial creatures in the affected area must attempt a Reflex save to avoid stumbling as the shockwave shakes the ground. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus the creature takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The area increases by 5 feet (to a 20-foot cone at 2nd rank, and so on).
name: Shockwave

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Show The Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=2367)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=43" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 260

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 8 hours
</row>

</column>

---

You and allies in the area gain preternatural knowledge of the path ahead, allowing you to intuit the best way forward and avoid potential obstacles, such as difficult or confusing terrain. For the purpose of long-distance overland [travel](/Rules.aspx?ID=2580) during exploration mode, traveling through difficult terrain reduces you to only three-quarters your travel Speed instead of half, and traveling through greater difficult terrain reduces your travel Speed to only half your travel Speed instead of one-third. Show the way doesn’t prevent you from falling into traps or encountering other artificial obstacles and hazards.

---
**Heightened (6th)** For the purpose of long-distance overland travel during exploration mode, traveling through difficult terrain doesn’t reduce your travel Speed at all, and traveling through greater difficult terrain reduces your travel Speed to only three-quarters of its normal value instead of one-third.
name: Show The Way

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Slough Skin](/Spells.aspx?ID=2368)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 260

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deities**
[Diomazul](/Deities.aspx?ID=614), [Narakaas](/Deities.aspx?ID=672), [Urazra](/Deities.aspx?ID=499), [Vildeis](/Deities.aspx?ID=487), [Yig](/Deities.aspx?ID=579)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

You continually and harmlessly slough off the top layer of your skin while new skin regenerates immediately, quickly moving damaging substances away from your body. The flat check to remove [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) from effects that coat your skin (such as most persistent acid damage) is reduced to 5, and you gain a +2 status bonus to your initial save against contact [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) (but not to further saves since, by that point, the toxin has already entered your system). If you’re affected by an effect other than persistent damage that depends on continuous contact with your skin, and if that effect allows a saving throw, you receive a new saving throw against that effect at the end of each turn when you attempt your flat checks against persistent damage, and you also receive a +2 status bonus to those saving throws. 

While affected by this spell, your continually shedding skin makes you much easier to Track. Anyone [Tracking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) you gains a +2 circumstance bonus to do so, and you can’t [Hide Your Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2407).
name: Slough Skin

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Temporary Tool](/Spells.aspx?ID=2369)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 260

**Tradition**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

**Deities**
[Amaznen](/Deities.aspx?ID=404), [Bharnarol](/Deities.aspx?ID=467), [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=333), [Findeladlara](/Deities.aspx?ID=461), [Magrim](/Deities.aspx?ID=450), [Ptah](/Deities.aspx?ID=638), [Wheels of Innovation](/Deities.aspx?ID=385), [Zogmugot](/Deities.aspx?ID=508)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute or until used
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure a temporary simple [tool](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2759), such as a shovel or [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2748) into your hands. It lasts until it’s used for a single activity or for 1 minute, whichever comes first, after which it disappears. The tool is obviously temporarily conjured and thus can’t be sold or passed off as a genuine item.
name: Temporary Tool

markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 260 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Dispater](/Deities.aspx?ID=397), [Shivaska](/Deities.aspx?ID=436) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Reflex **Duration** 1 minute --- You use magical chains, vines, or other tethers to bind your target to you. The creature can still try to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), and it or others can break the tethers by attacking them (the tethers have AC 15 and 10 Hit Points). You must stay within 30 feet of the target while it’s tethered; moving more than 30 feet away from your target ends the spell. The target must attempt a Reflex save. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed as long as it’s within 30 feet of you.
**Failure** The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed and can’t move more than 30 feet away from you until it Escapes or the spell ends.
**Critical Failure** The target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until it Escapes or the spell ends. --- **Heightened (+1)** The tethers’ AC increases by 3, and their Hit Points increase by 10.
name: Tether

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Restricted">
[Time Beacon](/Spells.aspx?ID=2371)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=2609)**

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 260, [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 183

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Organized play does not allow this spell. Instead, substitute [_true target_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726)._

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Shyka](/Deities.aspx?ID=460), [Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=580)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your turn
</row>

</column>

---

You create a beacon in time to return to it if things go wrong. You can cast _time beacon_ only on your turn. Keep careful track of everything that happens this turn after you cast time beacon. At the end of your turn, you can choose to rewind time back to just after you cast _time beacon_, removing all effects of your turn since then. [Curses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2766), traps, and other harmful effects that happen during your turn might prevent you from returning to the beacon if they’re powerful enough. If you were affected by any harmful effects during your turn after casting _time beacon_, in order to return to your beacon, _time beacon_ attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) check against each such effect. If it fails at any of these checks, you can’t return. After returning to the _time beacon_, the spell ends.
name: Time Beacon

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Wall of Radiance](/Spells.aspx?ID=2372)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 261

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deity**
[Sturovenen](/Deities.aspx?ID=523)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Fortitude

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a thin wall of brilliant light in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high. You must create the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don’t pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. The wall stands vertically and, if you wish, can be of a shorter length or height. Creatures adjacent to the wall are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65). Creatures that move through the wall take 2d6 fire damage (basic Fortitude save) as the concentrated light burns them.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) makes it difficult to discern creatures on the other side. Creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to other creatures on the opposite side of the wall from them. The fire damage increases to 3d6.
name: Wall of Radiance

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Wall of Shadow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2373)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Darkness" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=39" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 261

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Aleth](/Deities.aspx?ID=328), [Children of the Night](/Deities.aspx?ID=376)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You form a wall of pure darkness in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high. You must create the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don’t pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. The wall stands vertically and, if you wish, can be of a shorter length or height. The wall prevents light from passing through and appears as a sheet of pure darkness to creatures observing it. Creatures without [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410) or those unable to see through darkness can’t see creatures on the other side of the wall. The wall is too thin for creatures to Hide in the darkness itself, but creatures can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) from creatures on the other side of the wall as normal.

---
**Heightened (5th)** Creatures with darkvision (but not greater darkvision) can barely see through the wall. They treat targets seen through the wall as [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62).

**Heightened (7th)** Creatures with greater darkvision can barely see through the darkness. They treat targets seen through the wall as concealed. All other creatures are unable to see through the darkness at all.
name: Wall of Shadow

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Whirling Scarves](/Spells.aspx?ID=2374)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 261

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You surround yourself in a vortex of whirling colorful scarves of force that obfuscate you and disorient your foes. You gain the benefits of the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition, but only against ranged and melee attacks. When a melee attack fails to hit you because of the flat check for the concealed condition, the scarves snag the weapon or unarmed attack, and the creature takes a –1 circumstance penalty to further attacks with that weapon or unarmed attack until the end of its turn (or the end of its next turn, if it wasn’t the creature’s turn). The timing of the scarves’ movement is harder to predict for ranged attackers, so the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) for the concealed condition against ranged attacks increases from DC 5 to DC 6. You can Dismiss this spell.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The circumstance penalty to further attacks with a melee weapon or unarmed attack the scarves snag increases by 1. The DC of the flat check for the concealed condition against ranged attacks increases by 1.
name: Whirling Scarves

markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 262 **Domain** [Abomination](/Domains.aspx?ID=101) **Area** 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- With a wave of the hand, you cause all creatures in the area to see the world around them as if a gauzy film had been lifted away to expose the truth that lies below their perceived reality. Natural features they can see grow twisted and horrendous, structures appear distorted and ruined, and objects seem warped and alien. This alternate vision fades quickly, but the glimpse beyond the natural world lingers for the spell’s duration. [Aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) or worshippers of [Outer Gods or Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13) are bolstered by this vision of warped reality and gain a +1 status bonus to Will saving throws and attack rolls for 1 minute, while all other creatures must attempt a Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute and [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 1 for 1 minute and frightened 2. It can’t reduce the value of its frightened condition below 1 as long as it remains stupefied by this spell. --- **Heightened (+2)** The area increases by 5 feet.
name: Lift Nature's Caul

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 262, [Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=81) pg. 65 **Domain** [Abomination](/Domains.aspx?ID=101) **Trigger** A creature within range becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). **Range** 30 feet **Target** the triggering creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You open your mouth wide and inhale sharply; you draw in the bravery, self-confidence, and hope to which the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) target clings and then strip away the target’s false assumption that these fragile emotions can save them from the oblivion of terror. You deal 6d4 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage to the creature, which must attempt a Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage; you regain Hit Points equal to half the damage the target takes.
**Critical Failure** The creature’s frightened condition increases by 1 (to a maximum of frightened 4) and it takes double damage; you regain Hit Points equal to half the damage the target takes. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4.
name: Fearful Feast

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 262 **Domain** [Change](/Domains.aspx?ID=102) **Duration** 1 minute --- You make subtle changes to your body to adapt to the situation. Choose one of the following options that applies to you.
  • If you are in water, you gain a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) equal to half your Speed.
  • If you are in water, you become able to breathe water.
  • If it’s too dark for you to see, you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410).
  • If you are in an environment of [severe heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) or severe cold, you are protected from the effects of severe heat or severe cold, and the spell lasts 10 minutes.
name: Adapt Self

<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 262

**Domain**
[Change](/Domains.aspx?ID=102)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You take [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage.

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You shift yourself to adapt to the mystical wavelengths of the energy damage, protecting yourself from further harm. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to the triggering energy type; you gain this resistance after you take the triggering damage, so it applies only to future damage you take during the duration. 

---
**Heightened (+2)** The resistance increases by 5.
name: Adaptive Ablation

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 263 **Domain** [Cold](/Domains.aspx?ID=103) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** AC --- You fling a hollow icicle filled with winter’s wrath. Make a [spell attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2248) against the target’s AC. The bolt deals 1d8 piercing damage and lodges in the target. At the end of the target’s next turn, the bolt shatters, releasing a whirl of snow and ice that deals 1d12 [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage to the target and all adjacent creatures. The bolt can be removed with an Interact action, which causes it to melt harmlessly without detonating. **Critical Success** The initial bolt deals full damage and is especially well anchored, taking 2 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions to remove. The bolt’s explosion deals double damage.
**Success** The bolt and its explosion deal full damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The bolt’s initial damage increases by 1d8 and the explosion damage increases by 1d12.
name: Winter Bolt

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 263

**Domain**
[Cold](/Domains.aspx?ID=103)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) fortitude

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You supercool the nearby air, forming a cloud of dancing ice crystals in an emanation around you. A creature other than you that enters the area or ends its turn within the area takes 2d6 [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage. A creature can take this damage only once per turn. The crystals scatter and refract light; if a light effect enters diamond dust’s area, any creatures targeted by that effect gain a +2 status bonus to AC and saves against it. While the spell persists, you can activate or deactivate this aura by [Sustaining](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this spell.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Diamond Dust

markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 263 **Domain** [Decay](/Domains.aspx?ID=104) **Range** touch **Target** 1 creature or object **Defense** AC --- Your touch rots organic material and decays objects. Make a spell attack against the target’s AC. Your touch deals 1d12 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage plus 1d4 [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). If a creature uses an item to block withering grasp, such as with the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction, the item is automatically affected, but the creature doesn’t take damage (even if there is damage left over after the shield’s [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183)). Unlike normal void damage, the void damage from _withering grasp_ damages [objects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2363), [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=563), and the like by eroding away their substance. **Critical Success** Your touch deals double damage (both initial and persistent). If you target an object, lower its Hardness by 4 for 1 minute.
**Success** Your touch deals full damage. If you target an object, lower its Hardness by 2 for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d12 and the persistent damage increases by 1. If you target an object, lower its Hardness by an additional 1 point on both a success and a critical success.
name: Withering Grasp

When a creature within the area would regain Hit Points, such as from a healing effect or an ability like [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) or [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72), it must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The creature regains the full number of Hit Points that would be restored by the healing effect and is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature regains the full number of Hit Points that would be restored.
**Failure** The creature regains only half the number of Hit Points that would be restored.
**Critical Failure** The creature doesn’t regain Hit Points from the healing effect.
name: Fallow Field

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 264 **Domain** [Disorientation](/Domains.aspx?ID=105) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You sharpen a creature’s senses, though more distant objects become hazy, indistinct, or muted to it. The target gains a +1 status bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks attempted with any of its precise senses. Furthermore, any imprecise senses the target has are sharpened to [precise senses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2406) (though they don’t receive the status bonus). However, the target can’t perceive anything beyond a range of 20 feet with any of its senses. The duration is determined by the target’s Will save. You can allow allies to choose the outcome instead of attempting a saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is affected for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is affected for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target can’t perceive anything beyond a range of 10 feet. --- **Heightened (3rd)** You can target up to 2 creatures. **Heightened (6th)** You can target up to 4 creatures.
name: Clouded Focus

markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 264 **Domain** [Disorientation](/Domains.aspx?ID=105) **Range** 60 feet **Area** four 10-foot-by–10-foot spaces **Duration** 1 minute --- You create illusory hazards, such as spinning blades or a puddle of acid, in four 10-foot-by–10-foot spaces within range. The hazards are merely a mental projection, and a creature receives a Will save each time it touches a hazard or is occupying one’s space at the start of its turn. Depending on the result, the creature takes 4d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and might have difficulty moving through the area. A creature can take this damage only once per turn, even if it moves through several hazards. Choose bludgeoning, slashing, piercing, [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage when you Cast ephemeral hazards; resistances and weaknesses to those damage types apply if the target thinks they do, as judged by the GM. You can freely choose the appearance and damage type of each hazard as long as its appearance reflects the type of damage it deals (for instance, a [hazard](/Rules.aspx?ID=2576) that deals piercing damage might take the form of sharpened spikes). **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected by the hazards and no longer needs to attempt Will saves against them.
**Success** The creature realizes the hazards aren’t real but still takes half damage from them; on future Will saves against the hazards, the creature continues to use this result unless it rolls a critical success.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and treats the square as difficult terrain.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and can’t pass through a square containing a hazard. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Ephemeral Hazards

<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 264

**Domain**
[Dragon](/Domains.aspx?ID=106)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You shape energy into a small group of tiny dragons (or other serpentine creatures) that flit around you. Choose [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), or [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage when you [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734). For the duration of the spell, your Strikes with weapons or unarmed attacks deal 1 additional damage of the chosen type, as the dragons add their energy to your attacks. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell to change the damage type. In addition, you can Sustain the spell to have the dragons fly off to bombard a creature within 60 feet. That creature takes 2d4 damage of the chosen type (basic Reflex save). Once the dragons have been used in this way, they wink out of existence and the spell ends.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The additional amount of damage from the dragons increases by 1 and the damage dealt by the dragons' bombardment increases by 2d4.
name: Draconic Barrage

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 265 **Domain** [Dragon](/Domains.aspx?ID=106) **Area** 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You channel the might of dragons into your voice, letting out a roar that engenders respect in dragonkind but that instills fear in most other creatures. The impressive roar grants you a +2 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks for 10 minutes against dragons that were in the area at the time of casting. _Roar of the dragon_ affects non-dragon creatures with deep ties to dragonkind (such as a [barbarian](/Classes.aspx?ID=57) with the [draconic instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=9), a sorcerer with the [draconic bloodline](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=23), or a member of a culture that reveres dragons) as if they had the [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=581) trait. The GM decides if a creature is aligned enough with dragonkind to be affected in this way. All enemies within the area other than dragons must attempt a Will save; to these enemies, _roar of the dragon_ is a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round.
name: Roar of the Dragon

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 265 **Domain** [Dust](/Domains.aspx?ID=107) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** varies --- Dry winds dehydrate a creature. The target must attempt a Fortitude save; if used on a creature that has the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait or a creature made primarily of liquid (such as many [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453)), the creature uses the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result. Creatures whose bodies contain no significant moisture (such as [earth elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=409)) are immune to parch. If a creature affected by _parch_ drinks a liquid, such as a swig of water or a potion, the spell ends. Affected creatures that have the water trait or are made primarily of liquid, as well as affected creatures that can’t drink, can end the effect as a two-action activity, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes a –1 status penalty to its checks and DCs for 1 round.
**Failure** The target takes a –1 status penalty to its checks and DCs for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target takes a –2 status penalty to its checks and DCs for 1 minute and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) as long as it has this penalty. --- **Heightened (4th)** You can target up to 4 creatures.
name: Parch

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Dust Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=2388)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Nonlethal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=188" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 265

**Domain**
[Dust](/Domains.aspx?ID=107)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You call forth a swirling storm of dust. The dust storm obscures vision, as the effects of mist. The dust also makes the air unbreathable; creatures in the area must [hold their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439), though wearing a scarf or similar clothing over the mouth increases the time the creature can hold its breath by 5 rounds. Creatures entering or starting their turn in the dust storm take 1d6 slashing damage. Creatures that have the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait or that are made primarily of liquid take double damage.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Dust Storm

[Swear Oath](/Spells.aspx?ID=2389)
<actions string="Single Action or Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 265

**Domain**
[Duty](/Domains.aspx?ID=108)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

You loudly and firmly state a course of action that you swear to fulfill. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), [Ready](/Actions.aspx?ID=2299) a single action or free action you can use. If the action is in line with your personal edicts or the edicts presented by your [religion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2976), you can Cast this Spell as a single action. If the trigger you designate occurs and you can use the chosen action as a reaction, you gain a +1 status bonus to any attack roll or skill check required. If the trigger you designate doesn’t occur, you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell on your next turn to regain the Focus Point spent for this spell.

---
**Heightened (5th)** The status bonus is +2.

**Heightened (9th)** The status bonus is +3.
name: Swear Oath

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Dutiful Challenge](/Spells.aspx?ID=2390)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 265

**Domain**
[Duty](/Domains.aspx?ID=108)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Will

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

As you challenge an enemy, you take some of its attention away from your allies. The target takes a –1 status penalty to attack rolls, damage rolls, and skill checks against creatures other than you, and you take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls, damage rolls, and skill checks against creatures other than the target. Each time a creature other than you uses a hostile action that affects the challenged enemy, the enemy can attempt a Will save. On a success, the enemy no longer takes the penalty against that creature. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell, and it ends automatically if you or the target is defeated

---
**Heightened (+3)** The status penalties increase by 1.
name: Dutiful Challenge

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Redact](/Spells.aspx?ID=2391)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 266

**Domain**
[Glyph](/Domains.aspx?ID=109)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** unattended written materials of up to 1 Bulk
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 day
</row>

</column>

---

You strike mention of a specific name from one or more documents. When you [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), give one proper name, be it a creature, location, or object. Any mention of that name within the document becomes blurred, smudged, or otherwise completely illegible. Epithets and unambiguous references to the name are redacted as well; for instance, redacting the name of the goddess [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292) from a book would also remove references to her title “the Dawnflower.” Attempts to use this reference material to [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638) about the given proper name automatically fail.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** Instead of striking out a given name, you can replace it with a different name you choose. Epithets and references are substituted with similar ones relating to the replacement name. Anyone reading the text can attempt a Perception or Society check against your spell DC to notice the altered text, though that doesn’t tell them what the original said.

**Heightened (4th)** At your choice, the duration is unlimited.
name: Redact

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Ghostly Transcription](/Spells.aspx?ID=2392)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 266

**Domain**
[Glyph](/Domains.aspx?ID=109)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Glowing letters assemble in the air, providing a transcription of the target’s speech in all languages you speak. For the spell’s duration, any words spoken aloud by the target are spelled out in letters in front of them. When you [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), you can choose whether these letters are visible to all creatures or only certain creatures you specify. Any words the target mouths silently are similarly spelled out, allowing the target to communicate silently or in areas where sound doesn’t carry. This even allows the target to utter the incantations necessary for spells in such areas, but only if you choose to make the letters visible to all creatures. Furthermore, any of the target’s [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) abilities can affect creatures even in areas where sound doesn’t carry as long as you have made the glyphs visible to them and they have line of sight to the target; in this case, they gain the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell.
name: Ghostly Transcription

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Guided Introspection](/Spells.aspx?ID=2393)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 266

**Domain**
[Introspection](/Domains.aspx?ID=110)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You guide the target in examining their innermost feelings, their behaviors, and the motivations behind their actions, granting them clarity and insight. The target gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) and [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598). If the target would become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) during the spell’s duration, they reduce the duration of the confused condition by 1 round, to a minimum of 1 round. If the target would become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94), they reduce the value of the frightened or stupefied condition by 1, to a minimum of 1. Once any of these conditions occur, the spell ends.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The status bonus increases to +2.
name: Guided Introspection

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<row gap="medium">

[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) will

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute

</column>

---

You show the target the spiraling web of actions and consequences that is their life and the changes wrought by these decisions upon themselves and those around them. These events swirl around the target like shards of broken pottery. When you [Cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) or [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this Spell, you force the target to confront an alternate permutation of themselves, witnessing the events of their life as if they’d chosen another path. They take 4d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage (basic Will save) and are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the beginning of your next turn. On a critical success, the spell ends.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Confront Selves

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 267 **Domain** [Lightning](/Domains.aspx?ID=111) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** AC --- You fire a javelin of electricity that leaves a charged field around its target. Make a [spell attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2248) against the target’s AC. The javelin deals 1d6 electricity damage and 1 [persistent electricity damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). As long as the target is taking persistent damage from this spell, creatures gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls with [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) weapons or [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) effects against the target, and the target takes a –1 status penalty to saves against electricity effects. **Critical Success** The javelin deals double damage, both initial and persistent.
**Success** The javelin deals full damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The initial damage increases by 1d6, and the persistent damage increases by 1.
name: Charged Javelin

[Bottle the Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=2396)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 267

**Domain**
[Lightning](/Domains.aspx?ID=111)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You would take [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) damage.

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When lightning is turned against you, you store the charge safely within yourself, unleashing it when the time is right. You gain [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 against the triggering effect. If you successfully prevent damage in this way, at any time during the spell’s duration, you can spend a single action, which has the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) trait, to expend the charge and make a spell attack against the AC of a target within 60 feet. This attack deals electricity damage equal to the damage prevented by bottle the storm. If you Cast _bottle the storm_ a second time before the original duration elapses, or if you do not make this attack within 1 minute, the stored charge dissipates harmlessly.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The resistance increases to 15, and you can immediately make the spell attack granted by _bottle the storm_ as part of the reaction used to Cast the Spell.

**Heightened (10th)** The resistance increases to 20, and you can immediately make the spell attack granted by _bottle the storm_ as part of the reaction used to Cast the Spell.
name: Bottle the Storm

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 267 **Domain** [Naga](/Domains.aspx?ID=113) **Trigger** A creature within range attempts a Deception check to Lie or Feint. **Range** 30 feet **Target** the triggering creature **Defense** Will --- You unleash some choice words empowered with divine might. The creature takes 2d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes double damage and is stupefied 2 for 1d4 rounds. The creature can immediately choose to reveal the truth about its deception as a free action to immediately recover Hit Points equal to the mental damage it took and lose the stupefied condition from the spell. If it does, it quickly notes that its words and actions were lies, though it might need more time to explain the proper truth. If the creature used Deception to successfully Feint and it reveals the truth in this way, the target of the creature’s Feint is no longer [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) due to the successful Feint. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6.
name: Chastising Retort

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Show the Path](/Spells.aspx?ID=2400)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 267

**Domain**
[Naga](/Domains.aspx?ID=113)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You grant the target insight on things to come, guiding them toward a fate they find most fitting and encouraging them to continue this journey. The creature rolls a d20 at the start of each of its turns. The creature can use the result of the roll instead of rolling for any check before the start of their next turn. If the creature does substitute the roll in this way, they gain a +1 status bonus to whatever check they substitute on the following turn. Repeated substitutions increase the status bonus by 1, up to a total of +2. If the creature begins their turn without having substituted a roll in the previous round, the spell ends.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The status bonus can increase up to a total of +3.

**Heightened (10th)** The status bonus can increase up to a total of +4.
name: Show the Path

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 268 **Domain** [Nothingness](/Domains.aspx?ID=114) **Range** 5 feet **Area** 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** 1 minute --- Striking at thin air, you create hairline cracks in an unoccupied adjacent space that lead somewhere outside reality. Air rushes through the cracks, drawing Large or smaller creatures and objects of light Bulk or less toward the center. Large or smaller creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude save at the start of their turn; creatures that move into the area must attempt the save upon entering. The cracks of the door to beyond are too thin for anything to fully slip through, but decompressive effects deal 4d6 slashing damage to any creature or object that ends its turn adjacent to the door. You are unaffected by your own door to beyond. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is pulled 10 feet toward the door.
**Critical Failure** The creature is pulled 20 feet toward the door. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Door to Beyond

The condition value for the clumsy, enfeebled, or stupefied condition is 2 instead of 1.

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Font of Serenity](/Spells.aspx?ID=2406)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 268

**Domain**
[Repose](/Domains.aspx?ID=116)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

A divine beacon radiates serenity, soothing nearby allies. Allies within the area gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects. An ally can touch the font at the center of the burst as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action; if they do so, the spell attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) one emotion effect affecting the ally of the ally’s choice, after which that emotion effect is temporarily immune for 1 day. If the spell succeeds at the counteract check, it removes the emotion effect from your ally, though not from any other creatures the effect might be affecting.

---
**Heightened (+4)** The bonus increases by 1.
name: Font of Serenity

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 269

**Domain**
[Sorrow](/Domains.aspx?ID=117)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30-foot  [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) will

</row>

</column>

---

You let out your negative emotions in a guttural wail that shakes your enemies’ hearts and deals 1d8 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage (basic Will save) to each creature in the area. If you have any harmful conditions imposed by [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects, lament deals additional mental damage equal to the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) rank of the highest-level effect. 

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d8.
name: Lament

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 269 **Domain** [Sorrow](/Domains.aspx?ID=117) **Area** 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) **Defense** Will **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- Sadness flows out of you into nearby creatures, blotting out any other thoughts they had. The first time a creature begins its turn in the area or enters the area, it must attempt a Will save. If it later leaves and reenters the area, it uses the same effect as before. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –1 status penalty to skill checks and [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks as long as it remains in the area.
**Failure** While the creature is in the area, any emotion effects of lower counteract rank than overflowing sorrow are suppressed, and whenever the creature attempts to use an emotion action or cast an emotion spell, it must succeed at a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or the action or spell is disrupted.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature can’t use emotion actions or spells. --- **Heightened (+2)** When you [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), you can choose to increase the area by 5 feet.
name: Overflowing Sorrow

markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 269 **Domain** [Soul](/Domains.aspx?ID=118) **Range** touch **Target** 1 living creature that has a soul **Defense** Fortitude --- Your touch disrupts the connection between body and soul, possibly forcing the soul out of the body altogether. The target must attempt a Fortitude save. You can allow allies to choose the degree of success instead of rolling a saving throw. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 as its soul briefly loses its connection to their body.
**Critical Failure** The target’s soul exits its body and the target’s body becomes [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) for 1 round. While their body is stunned in this way, the target remains fully aware in soul form; they simply can’t consciously move their body (though the body makes basic instinctual defensive movements). The target’s soul has the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) trait, is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), and has a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 60 feet. It can’t [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [cast spells](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), or attempt any skill checks that require a physical body, and it must always maintain line of effect to the target’s body. When the target’s body ceases being stunned, the target’s soul instantly returns to their body as the target wakes. --- **Heightened (+3)** If a willing ally chooses to critically fail, they can stay in soul form for 1 additional round.
name: Eject Soul

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Ectoplasmic Interstice](/Spells.aspx?ID=2410)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 269

**Domain**
[Soul](/Domains.aspx?ID=118)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You force the mortal [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) and [Ethereal Planes](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) to partially merge, creating a zone that causes incorporeal and spiritual entities to take on many of the aspects of corporeal creatures. Within the area of effect, [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures can’t pass through solid objects or corporeal creatures, and they can manipulate and attempt Strength-based checks against physical creatures and objects. An incorporeal PC or other creature with a normal Strength modifier simply uses that modifier, but if the creature had a Strength modifier of –5, like most incorporeal monsters, it uses a +4 modifier instead, increasing its [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) modifier by 9 accordingly. Attacks against creatures that are within the area overcome [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) as if they were by [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) weapons.
name: Ectoplasmic Interstice

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 269 **Domain** [Star](/Domains.aspx?ID=119) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** 1 day --- You call a tiny star to orbit a creature in a sparkling halo before shooting up into the heavens, where it marks the creature’s rough location. The target must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is dazzled for 1 round. While the spell persists, as long as you can see the night sky and the target is visible from the same night sky, you can sense the approximate direction and distance to the creature by [Sustaining](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell. This is enough to track the creature, but not pinpoint their exact square (for instance, to make an attack). You can set a number of zenith stars equal to your Wisdom modifier; if you exceed this number, your oldest zenith star is automatically [Dismissed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round. --- **Heightened (+1)** The duration increases by 1 day. **Heightened (4th)** You ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition against targets marked by zenith star.
name: Zenith Star

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Asterism](/Spells.aspx?ID=2412)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 269

**Domain**
[Star](/Domains.aspx?ID=119)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 150 feet

**Area** five 30-foot [lines](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) reflex

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Lines of burning starlight form a constellation around you. Draw five lines, one at a time; each line must anchor at you or a creature or object already connected to a previous line, and it must end at a creature or object within 30 feet of its anchor (possibly allowing the asterism to extend to its full 150-foot range). A creature that begins its turn in one of these lines or enters one of these lines on its turn takes 4d6 [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage (basic Reflex save). A creature can take this damage only once per turn, even if it moves through several lines. Targets that are part of the asterism do not take damage from it. If the distance between two targets (or you and a target) ever exceeds 30 feet, the line of starlight between the two breaks, and any lines that now no longer have a direct path back to you also break. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Asterism

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Swarmsense](/Spells.aspx?ID=2413)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 270

**Domain**
[Swarm](/Domains.aspx?ID=120)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained
</row>

</column>

---

You extend your senses through a multitude of crawling and flying creatures. You create a crawling swarm in your square. You can hear through the swarm as if using your normal auditory senses. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) and the first time you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) it each round, you can move the swarm 10 feet along the ground in any direction. The swarm has AC 15 and a +0 bonus to its saves. Any damage dealt to the swarm destroys it and ends the spell.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The swarm has a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) of 10 feet.

**Heightened (5th)** The swarm has a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 10 feet. You can see through the swarm using your visual senses.

**Heightened (7th)** The swarm gains a 10-foot status bonus to its Speeds.
name: Swarmsense

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Swarm Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=2414)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 270

**Domain**
[Swarm](/Domains.aspx?ID=120)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 5 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You discorporate into a swarm of Tiny creatures. While in swarm form, you are the same size, you have the [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=707) trait and you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to slashing and piercing damage and weakness 5 to area and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage. You can fit into spaces only a few inches wide, moving your constituent creatures through the gap, and you can share a space with another creature. You don’t gain the [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78) ability, so you are still affected normally by mental effects. As a swarm, you can’t speak, [cast spells](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) actions requiring your hands, [activate your magic items](/Actions.aspx?ID=100), or make any of your [Strikes](/Rules.aspx?ID=2895) with your normal body. While in swarm form, you can crawl all over any creature that shares your space as a single action. That creature must attempt a Fortitude save against your spell DC or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure). You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the Spell.

---
**Heightened (+2)** Your resistances and weaknesses each increase by 5.
name: Swarm Form

**Trigger** A creature or object within range is hit by an [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540).

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** the triggering creature or object
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 round
</row>

</column>

---

You transpose the moment that the target would be injured to a point later in its timestream. Roll damage normally, but do not reduce Hit Points or apply any other effects of the attack until the spell’s duration has elapsed. When the spell ends, the target is immediately affected by the attack, taking damage and being affected by any additional effects. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this spell.
name: Delay Consequence

markdown:
**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 270 **Domain** [Time](/Domains.aspx?ID=121) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature or up to 1 Bulk of objects **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- The flow of time congeals around an object or creature, holding it in place. The target must attempt a Will save (an unattended object automatically critically fails its save). **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 as time thickens around it.
**Failure** Time ceases to pass for the target for 1 round. It is invulnerable to all damage, it can’t be targeted or affected by anything, and no rounds elapse for any timed durations, conditions, afflictions, and other effects it has. While in stasis, the target can’t be moved, and it remains fixed in place, defying gravity if need be.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is held in stasis for 3 rounds. At the end of each of its turns, it can attempt a Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round or end it entirely on a critical success.
name: Stasis

</row>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target gains a +2 status bonus to the triggering check, or a +3 bonus if the target has master or better proficiency in the skill. This bonus can change the outcome of the target’s skill check.

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You suppress your choice of one of the following conditions that’s affecting the target: [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). The duration of the condition doesn’t elapse while it is suppressed, so if you don’t remove the effect that caused the condition, the condition returns after the spell’s duration expires.

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Object Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=2419)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 271

**Domain**
[Vigil](/Domains.aspx?ID=123)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Target** 1 weapon or tool
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

By touching an object, you draw forth the experience of those who created and used it. If you touch a weapon, your proficiency rank with that weapon temporarily increases to trained (if it is not already trained or better). If you touch a [tool](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2759), your proficiency rank with the next skill check you attempt with that tool (such as a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check with a blacksmith’s hammer) within the spell’s duration temporarily increases to trained (if it is not already trained or better). This spell has no benefit for activities that take longer than a minute to complete.

---
**Heightened (6th)** Your proficiency rank increases to expert instead of trained.
name: Object Memory

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Remember the Lost](/Spells.aspx?ID=2420)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 271

**Domain**
[Vigil](/Domains.aspx?ID=123)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You call upon the lost and forgotten, assailing your foes’ minds with the memories of those who died with a grievance toward them. Enemies in the area take 6d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage (basic Will save) and are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 on a critical failure. If you know the names of anyone murdered or grievously wronged by an enemy in the area, you can chant those victims’ names when you [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) to improve the clarity of the visions, increasing the damage to the corresponding enemy from 6d6 to 6d10; you can do so for multiple enemies if you know specific victims of each enemy. The visions are personal to each foe in the area, and you can’t use this spell to discern a murderer by guessing a name. A creature that truly knows no one who died with any sort of grievance to that creature is [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to this effect.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6 (or 2d10 to an enemy when you name a specific victim).
name: Remember the Lost

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />

**Domain**
[Wood](/Domains.aspx?ID=124)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** wooden object or plant of at least 4 Bulk
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

All [wood](/Elements.aspx?ID=7), dead or alive, is your armory. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), you draw forth a +1 weapon of your choice from the target. The weapon must be 2 Bulk or less. This weapon is of the same material as the target.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The weapon is a _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829)_ weapon.

**Heightened (5th)** The weapon is a _+2 striking_ weapon.

**Heightened (7th)** The weapon is a _+3 greater striking_ weapon.
name: Arms Of Nature

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Wood Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=2422)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=26" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 271

**Domain**
[Wood](/Domains.aspx?ID=124)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Wood shapes itself according to your will readily, making staircases appear as you climb up the side of a tree, automatically weaving vines into ladders, or lowering branches to lift you up to the top. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), move up to your Speed in any direction or along any surface, so long as there are living plants or trees of size Large or larger within 15 feet of you. The wood allows you to move but doesn’t protect you from hazardous terrain or other dangers. Each time you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this spell, you can move again in the same way.

---
**Heightened (8th)** The duration of this spell becomes 10 minutes. You no longer need to Sustain the spell to extend its duration, but you can Sustain it to move as described.
name: Wood Walk

The ashes scour flesh and linger in the wounds, igniting upon the slightest flicker of flame. Creatures in the area take 4d6 slashing damage and must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and has [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 3 until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and has weakness to fire 3 for 1 minute. If the creature is killed by fire during this time it’s reduced to ashes, though its gear remains.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature takes double damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The slashing damage increases by 2d6 and weakness to fire increases by 1.
name: Incendiary Ashes

<title level="1" right="Focus 6" pfs="Standard">
[Ash Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=2425)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Oracle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=319" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You take the form of a cloud of minuscule ash particles. You gain [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 3, [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to piercing and slashing damage, and immunity to [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), falling damage, and precision damage. You can’t move through solid objects, but you can stream through tiny gaps as if they were [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). Any ability with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) trait that involves blowing or shifting wind (such as [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550)) deals you 1d6 bludgeoning damage per rank of the spell each round you are in its area, in addition to its normal effects.

---
**Heightened (9th)** The resistance increases to 15.
name: Ash Form

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 287 **Mystery** [Blight](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=21) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 living creature **Defense** Fortitude --- Weeping sores open on the target as you expose it to decay, dealing 1d4 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage and 1d4 [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) damage plus 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The target must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half the initial damage and no persistent bleed damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double initial damage and double persistent bleed damage. --- **Heightened (+2)** The void damage and poison damage each increase by 1d4, and the persistent bleed damage increases by 2.
name: Ulcerous Canker

<row gap="medium">

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

In the right dose, a scourge can become a cure. Deal [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) damage equal to the spell’s level to the target, then attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) check against one [disease](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767) or poison afflicting the target. The target is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. If the target is [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to poison, this spell has no effect.

---
**Heightened (5th)** You can attempt counteract checks against any number of poisons or diseases afflicting the target.
name: Purging Toxins

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 287 **Mystery** [Blight](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=21) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude --- You speed the natural process of a creature’s decline and debilitation. As its body withers, the target takes 9d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage and must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage and is your choice of [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1, [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1, or [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is clumsy 1, drained 1, and enfeebled 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is clumsy 1, drained 1, and enfeebled 1 for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (+1)** The void damage increases by 1d6.
name: Accelerated Decomposition

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=2613)** **Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 287, [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 189 **Mystery** [Time](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=23) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will --- You share some of your own temporal distortions with a foe, altering their mind and body unpredictably as they’re thrown backward or forward in time. Roll 1d4. On a 1, the foe becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61); on a 2, it becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71); on a 3, it becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94); and on a 4, you choose which condition applies. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is either clumsy 1, enfeebled 1, or stupefied 1 for 1 round, depending on the result of the d4.
**Failure** The target is either clumsy 1, enfeebled 1, or stupefied 1 for 4 rounds, depending on the result of the d4.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the time warp is stronger, increasing the condition’s effects but making it run its course faster. The condition’s value is 3, and the condition lasts for 2 rounds.
name: Temporal Distortion

You manipulate time around your ally, pushing them forward a few moments in time. The target is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) on its next turn and can use the action only to [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

---
**Heightened (6th)** You can affect up to four willing creatures other than yourself within range.

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=2615)** **Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 287, [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 189 **Mystery** [Time](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=23) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You cast a creature’s mind back through time, forcing them to take other paths and make other choices, experiencing countless alternate lives in an instant. The creature becomes overwhelmed with regret over paths untraveled, lives unlived, and times unrealized. The creature takes 6d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and must attempt a Will save. After the effect is resolved, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 day. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is stupefied 2 for 1 minute. While affected, the creature must attempt another save at the start of its turn; on a failure, it’s slowed 1 for that turn as it sobs uncontrollably.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and for 1 minute, it’s stupefied 4 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as it sobs uncontrollably. --- **Heightened (+1)** The mental damage increases by 1d6.
name: Manifold Lives
