<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Entreat Spirit](/Spells.aspx?ID=2432)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 295

<row gap="medium">

 

**Requirements** You must be Bonded with a Spirit
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You invite a spirit you’ve bonded with to bolster your mind. You gain a +1 status bonus to an Intelligence-, Wisdom-, or Charisma-based skill of your choice. Additionally, you gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), and [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The status bonus to the skill and your saving throws increases to +2.

**Heightened (8th)** The status bonus to the skill and your saving throws increases to +3.
name: Entreat Spirit
requirement_markdown: You must be Bonded with a Spirit

Additionally, creatures that critically fail the save are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d8.

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Share Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=2434)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Hex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=324" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=323" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 297

**Patron Theme**
[Choir Politic](/Patrons.aspx?ID=24)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

Your patron temporarily links your vision with that of another, causing both of you to be able to see through each other’s eyes. For the duration of the hex, the two of you share any vision-based senses (such as [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410) or [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411)) and gain a +2 status bonus to Perception checks, but if either of you are [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) or [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), you both are.

If you cast _share vision_ while a previous casting of this hex is still in effect, the previous effect ends.
name: Share Vision

The target takes damage only once from this spell, whether or not you Sustain it.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4 and the fast healing increases by 1.

markdown:
**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=2597)** **Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 298, [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 159 **Patron Theme** [The Unseen Broker](/Patrons.aspx?ID=26) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You offer to broker a pact of peace. If the target accepts and doesn’t take hostile actions against you and your allies during the hex’s duration, you take a –1 status penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to them. If they refuse and take a hostile action against you or an ally during the hex’s duration, they must first attempt a Will save. If they accepted the offer and then take a hostile action against you or an ally during the hex’s duration, they must first attempt a Will save and treat the result as one category worse. Regardless of the outcome, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 minute. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target takes 2d4 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and a –1 status penalty to attack and damage rolls against you and your allies for the hex’s duration.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the penalty is –2. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4.
name: Pact Broker

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Spirit Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=2437)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Hex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=324" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=323" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 299

**Patron Theme**
[Baba Yaga](/Patrons.aspx?ID=27)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 unattended object up to 1 Bulk
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Using a sliver of [Baba Yaga](/Patrons.aspx?ID=9)’s power, you briefly bring a nearby object to life. The object gains a means of locomotion if it does not already have one, such as sprouting chicken legs, and [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to 25 feet to a space you decide within range. Once per round, you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell to command the object further, either prompting it to move again or to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). If prompted to move, the object Strides up to 25 feet again to a space within 30 feet of you. If prompted to Strike, the object attacks one creature of your choice adjacent to its space. Make a melee [spell attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2248) roll against the creature. On a success, the creature takes 2d4 damage. The damage is either bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, as appropriate for the object. The object doesn’t do anything if it doesn’t receive further commands from you. If you cast _spirit object_ again, any previously affected object reverts back to normal. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell.

---
**Heightened (+1)** Increase the maximum Bulk of the target by 1 and the damage by 2d4.
name: Spirit Object

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Limited">
[Bee-Man's Summons](/Spells.aspx?ID=2439)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 80

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500-foot-radius [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) centered on you

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the next time you make your daily preparations or until discharged
</row>

</column>

---

You recreate the [Bee-Man’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3694) infamous ability to sense when someone utters their name. During the spell’s duration, you mentally sense whenever someone (referred to as a speaker) speaks your full name while within the spell’s area. You gain a vague sense of the speaker’s identity, such as “a local farmer” or “a halfling in distress,” unless the speaker is someone you have met and interacted with before, in which case you recognize the speaker specifically. As a reaction within 1 minute of the speaker’s utterance, you can send the speaker a telepathic prompt, asking if they intend to summon you. If they respond affirmatively, the spell’s remaining duration decreases to [sustained](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) (up to 10 minutes), during which time you know the direction to where the speaker named you and how far away they are.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The emanation’s radius increases to 1,000 feet.

**Heightened (7th)** The emanation’s radius increases to 1 mile, and instead of locating the speaker, you can instead converse with the speaker for 5 minutes. This otherwise works as [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665).

**Heightened (9th)** As 7th, except the emanation’s radius increases to 5 miles, and the duration of the conversation is 10 minutes.
name: Bee-Man's Summons

The hedge has AC 5, [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 5, and 20 Hit Points. It’s [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to critical hits and precision damage. **Critical Success** The creature escapes the hedge before it fully forms.

**Failure** The creature is trapped inside the hedge.
**Critical Failure** The creature is trapped inside the hedge, and the hedge’s Hit Points are increased by half. --- **Heightened (4th)** The hedge’s hardness increases to 7 and it’s Hit Points increase to 30. **Heightened (5th)** The hedge’s hardness increases to 9 and it’s Hit Points increase to 40. You can target a creature of Large size or smaller. **Heightened (6th)** The hedge’s hardness increases to 11 and its Hit Points increase to 50. You can target a creature of Large size or smaller. **Heightened (7th)** The hedge’s hardness increases to 13 and its Hit Points increase to 60. You can target a creature of Huge size or smaller **Heightened (8th)** The hedge’s hardness increases to 15 and its Hit Points increase to 70. You can target a creature of Huge size or smaller. **Heightened (9th)** The hedge’s hardness increases to 17 and its Hit Points increase to 80. You can target a creature of Huge size or smaller.

**Failure** The creature becomes dazzled for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes dazzled and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round. --- **Heightened (3rd)** The range increases to 90 feet and the area increases to a 20-foot-radius burst. **Heightened (6th)** The range increases to 120 feet and the area increases to a 30-foot-radius burst. **Heightened (9th)** The range increases to 150 feet, the area increases to a 40-foot-radius burst, and when a creature would become dazzled, they become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) instead.

May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 78 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 30 feet **Duration** 1 hour --- You conjure an intricate vehicle, carved entirely from wood, to serve as a method of conveyance. The [vehicle](/Rules.aspx?ID=3116) appears in an unoccupied area of your choice within range. The vehicle can be piloted using [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Driving Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) skill checks. The DC to pilot the vehicle and the DC of the vehicle’s collision are equal to your spell DC. The vehicle’s remaining statistics are presented below. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), choose whether to create a Large skiff, a Large wagon, or a Medium cycle. With the GM’s permission, you might instead summon a different vehicle of your choice with a maximum level of 1; this vehicle must be made primarily of [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) matter, have [common](/Traits.aspx?ID=557) rarity, and be Large or smaller. **Large Skiff—Space** 15 feet long, 5 feet wide, 3 feet high; **Crew** 1 pilot; **Passengers** 3; **Speed swim** 30 feet ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644))
**Large Wagon—Space** 10 feet long, 10 feet wide, 7 feet high; **Crew** 1 pilot; **Passengers** 3; **Speed** 35 feet (magical)
**Medium Cycle—Space** 5 feet long, 3 feet wide, 3 feet high; **Crew** 1 pilot; **Passengers** 0; **Speed** 40 feet (magical)
**AC** 13; **Fortitude** +8
**Hardness** 5, HP 40 (BT 20); **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage; **Weaknesses** fire 5, slashing 5
**Collision** 2d6 --- **Heightened (+1)** The vehicle’s AC increases by 2, Fortitude bonus increases by 2, Hardness increases by 1, HP increases by 20, and the collision damage increases by 1d6. In addition, the maximum level of vehicle you can summon with GM permission increases by 2. The duration increases by 1 hour.
name: Conjured Conveyance

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Wardens of Wildwood **Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 79 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** **Range** 30 feet **Saving Throw** Fortitude --- _Nethys Note: This spell was not printed with an action type._ You conjure a mote of cordyceps spores uniquely tailored to the target before whisking it at them. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), choose one of the following behaviors that the fungus compels: ascend, consume, descend, or lure. While the target is controlled by the cordyceps toxin’s stage 3 effects, it performs that behavior. This control might include risky behavior (such as climbing down a precarious cliff or weaving between armed foes), but it doesn’t compel outright lethal actions (such as leaping off the top of that same cliff). If the behavior directly leads to harm (such as falling off the cliff) or [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) (such as being attacked by creatures that can now reach the descending creature), the target gains a +4 bonus to their next saving throw against the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). **Ascend:** The creature tries to reach higher altitudes by any reasonable means, such as [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), seeking stairs, or even stacking debris to jump atop of in an attempt to be as high up as possible. **Consume:** The creature greedily eats or drinks whatever is nearby, using actions to draw and consume [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=589), food, or other [consumable](/Traits.aspx?ID=564) items. If the creature has a [jaws](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) Strike, fangs Strike, or similar unarmed Strike, the creature can instead chase after and use that Strike against edible targets. If no other food or drink is accessible, the creature attempts to steal or seek nearby nutrition. **Descend:** The creature tries to reach lower altitudes by any reasonable means, such as Climbing, descending while [flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or even falling [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and attempting to [burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) into the ground. **Lure:** The creature moves toward an exposed location and attempts to get bystanders’ attention, such as by gesticulating, [Performing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), or igniting light sources. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while controlled in this way. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is afflicted with cordyceps toxin at stage 1.
**Critical Failure** The target is afflicted with cordyceps toxin at stage 2. **Cordyceps Toxin** (poison) **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) (1 round); **Stage 3** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) (1 round)
name: Cordyceps Command

When you cast _reflected beauty_, choose a willing creature that’s the same size as you and that you can see within 30 feet. The spell then disguises you with a realistic illusion, as if via [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) heightened to 3rd rank, but includes tactile and olfactory sensation in addition to visual and voice. The appearance of the illusion that disguises you includes any changes to sex characteristics or other aspects needed to match the target creature’s heart’s desire, allowing you to interact with them as the person they could be. If you’re ever more than 30 feet from the subject you’re reflecting, _reflected beauty_ immediately ends. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this spell.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 83 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will --- You make the spell’s target believe they had something incredibly important to say but forgot to say it, and now they’ve missed their opportunity. Sensations of overwhelming panic akin to stage fright flood the target’s mind, causing them to suffer excruciating mental anguish and take 12d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage. The target might even become filled with the conviction that they’ve doomed themselves by missing their cue. The target must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The conviction of a missed cue is only a fleeting notion that passes quickly without any effect on the target.
**Success** The target takes half damage and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and becomes frightened 2. In addition, the target is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for as long as they remain frightened.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and becomes frightened 3. In addition, the target is slowed 1 for as long as they remain frightened. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6.
name: Missed Cue

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 83

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained (up to 1 minute)
</row>

</column>

---

With a quick series of wrist flicks, music fills the air. While the music can be heard as if it were performed normally at a distance, creatures in the spell’s area become affected in more significant ways. When you cast _musical shift_, select a key signature (to affect enemies) and a time signature (to affect allies) from the options below; all creatures within the area are affected as indicated. You can change the key signature or time signature as part of the action you take when you Sustain the spell. 

**Flat (key signature)** Whenever an enemy critically fails at a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), saving throw, or skill check, they fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) in addition to other effects from the critical failure. 

**Natural (key signature)** Enemies take a –2 status penalty to attack rolls.

**Sharp (key signature)** Whenever an enemy takes piercing or slashing damage, they also take 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). 

**Double (time signature)** You and your allies become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) and can use the extra action each round only for [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) actions. 

**Quadruple (time signature)** You and your allies gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls.

**Triple (time signature)** You and your allies gain a +2 status bonus to Armor Class and Reflex saving throws.
name: Musical Shift

The scene would play out exactly as you envisioned it, if only the actors would respect their blocking. You instantly transport the targeted creatures and any items they’re wearing and holding from their current space to an unoccupied space within range. You don’t need to be able to see the destinations as long as you’ve been there in the past and know its relative location and distance from each target. Creatures affected by _take your places_ are then temporarily immune to this spell for 1 minute.

May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 83 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Area** [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) up to 40 feet **Defense** Will **Duration** sustained (up to 1 minute) --- Your voice trills in perfect vibrato. When casting the spell, you can make the area any radius you choose, up to 40 feet. The reverberations of your voice continue to shimmer and hang in the air as long as you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell but can’t be heard at all outside of the area. A creature must attempt a Will save if it’s within the area when you [Cast the Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) or as soon as it enters the area while the spell is in effect. Once a creature has attempted the save, it uses the same result for that casting of _vibrant vibrato_. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 5d10 [persistent sonic damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) at the end of their turn as long as they remain within the aura’s area of effect.
**Failure** As success, but if the creature leaves the area, or if you move far enough from the creature that they’re no longer in the area, the creature hears a shattering sound and takes 5d10 [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. The creature is then [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for the rest of the spell’s duration.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes double the sonic damage and is stunned 3. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage on a failed save increases by 1d10.
name: Vibrant Vibrato

You bolster your ally with reminders of their physical prowess, granting them additional defenses against harmful [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects. The target gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws against mental effects and against effects that hinder movement (including those that reduce Speed or apply the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) conditions). This bonus increases to +2 if the source of the effect has the [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) trait.

Creatures in the area take 4d8 [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage and 4d8 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage. Each creature must attempt a Fortitude save. **Failure** The creature takes full damage and is deafened for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is permanently deafened. --- **Heightened (+1)** The sonic and void damage each increase by 1d8. The Hardness threshold of items destroyed by the spell increases by 1.
name: Utter Destruction

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 75 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will --- Tanglebriar has been called a “curse on the land,” which has inspired [Treerazer’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=324) cult to develop this notorious spell. The target must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** A fungal bloom springs up from the ground in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around the target. This area is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for creatures that enter the area. The target treats all terrain as difficult terrain since this swath of fungus moves with them as they do, transforming into a tangle of spores and floating tendrils if the target flies or a thick swath of stringy floating algae if the target swims. This effect ends after 1 minute or as soon as the curse is lifted, whichever comes first.
**Failure** As success, but the fungal bloom increases to 10 feet. It persists until the curse is lifted. In addition, the target is also affected by the spores exuded by the fungal bloom—roll 1d4 and consult the results below to see how the spores affect them. This affect reactivates automatically every 24 hours, replacing the previous result. **1:** The spores cause atrophy; the target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1. **2:** The spores cause fibrous fungal growths to sprout from the target; the target is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1. **3:** The spores settle in the target’s blood and flesh and cause great pain; the target is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1. **4:** The spores intrude upon the mind and cause hallucinations; the target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the emanation increases to 15 feet, and the condition value caused by the spores increases to 2.
name: Tanglecurse

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 75 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 60 feet **Target** one creature or object **Defense** AC and [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude --- You unleash a sickly gray beam of toxic spores at your target. Make a [spell attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2248) against the target. If you hit a non-magical object that’s made of organic material (such as a tree, wooden house, or massive skull), it melts away into a foul-smelling sludge. A single casting can destroy no more than a 10-foot cube of matter. If you hit a creature, it takes 6d12 [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) damage and 6d12 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save. If you critically hit, the target gets a result one degree of success worse than the outcome of its Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage. Fungal tendrils swiftly digest the body and reduce it to sludge—the target takes 2d12 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target takes double damage, plus 4d12 persistent acid damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The poison damage and spirit damage each increase by 1d12.
name: Ray of Corruption

[Dimensional Excision](/Spells.aspx?ID=2459)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 78

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will

</row>

</column>

---

You manipulate the boundaries between planes like a scalpel, using it to cut away portions of a creature’s essence and banishing those portions to other realities and dimensions, afflicting the target deep lacerations or even severed appendages. The creature takes 14d10 damage (no damage type) and 2d10 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and must attempt a Will save. If the target is not on its home plane, it takes a –4 status penalty to this save. A creature reduced to 0 HP has their entire body sectioned out and banished across multiple planes and dimensions, leaving nothing behind but their gear.

---
**Heightened (10th)** The base damage increases by 2d10, and the persistent bleed damage by 1d10.
name: Dimensional Excision

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 78 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** varies --- You create a cone of shimmering energy that attempts to banish the influence of invasive, supernatural blight, such as that found in Tanglebriar. Blighted [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) in the area becomes regular terrain, while blighted greater difficult terrain becomes difficult terrain; [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368) in the area becomes nonhazardous. These effects persist for 1 hour. Creatures in the area that carry features of this blight (as determined by the GM, but automatically including all creatures with the fiend trait in this adventure) are trimmed as well, their supernatural infusion of blight being drained. These creatures take 12d10 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage and must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature’s reach with attacks is reduced by 5 feet (to a minimum of 5 feet) for 1 hour, they are [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature’s reach is reduced to 5 feet for 1 hour, they are sickened 2 and slowed 1 for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (10th)** The spirit damage increases by 2d10, and 1 hour durations increase to 24 hours.
name: Trim the Blight

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Avenging Wildwood](/Spells.aspx?ID=2461)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 53

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Andoran have access to this option._

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Medium tree sprouts in an unoccupied square within range, its branches capable of striking out at your foes. This animated tree uses your spell attack modifier for its attack rolls and your spell DC for its AC. Its saving throw modifiers are equal to your spell DC – 10, and it has 20 Hit Points. Other creatures can’t occupy its space, but allies can pass through its space. When you [Cast the Spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) and each time you Sustain it, you can have the tree make a branches Strike against a creature within 15 feet of it, dealing 2d8 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. You choose the damage type each time.

If the tree is in soil and survives to the end of the spell’s duration, it remains as an ordinary, non-magical tree and continues to grow and thrive. The GM might determine that the tree disappears immediately in certain inhospitable situations.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The animated tree has 10 additional Hit Points, and its branches Strike deals 1d8 additional damage.
name: Avenging Wildwood

<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 53

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Andoran have access to this option._

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Fortitude

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a dome of brilliant light that destroys projectiles attempting to pass through it. Ammunition from physical ranged attacks—such as [arrows](/Weapons.aspx?ID=443), [bolts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=441), [sling bullets](/Weapons.aspx?ID=442), and other objects of similar size—is destroyed in a flash of light when it passes into or out of the globe’s area. Attackers targeting creatures or objects through the globe’s surface with physical ranged attacks must succeed at a Fortitude save or become dazzled for 1 round (blinded for 1 round on a critical failure).
name: Radiant Globe

markdown:
**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 75 **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- Coins, trinkets carved from heavy stone, and other cumbersome treasures rain down upon a foe. The target must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is encumbered for the duration of the spell.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the extra weight also knocks the target [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Unexpected Windfall

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Capital Dividend](/Spells.aspx?ID=2466)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not immediately killed.

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Through diet and other strict regimens, you’ve fortified your body as Kalistrade’s teachings have fortified your mind. You remain at 1 Hit Point, and your skin takes on a slight warm glow. 

For the next minute, any time you would regain Hit Points from a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) effect, this magic amplifies the effect, and you regain an additional 8 Hit Points.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The additional healing increases by 2 Hit Points.
name: Capital Dividend

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 98 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You assert your power over the target’s own spirit as you reach deep into its soul in order to discover a moment of excruciating anguish, debilitating humiliation, or abject despair. The target takes 12d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage and must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The target takes no damage, with a brief sensation of pain followed by a pleasant memory.
**Success** The target takes half damage and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and is frightened 2 and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d8.
name: Attacked From Within

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Defended By Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=2468)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=149" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 98

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 ally and 1 enemy
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You entreat a spirit or the spiritual energies in your location to surround and protect an ally from an certain foe. Each time the enemy attacks and damages the ally, the enemy takes 1d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. The enemy is aware of these spirits and has a general sense that attacking the ally will draw the spirits’ ire.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Defended By Spirits

markdown:
**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 98 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This option is limited for PFS play due to its powerful effect even on a successful save._ **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You open the target’s eyes to the plethora of spirits surrounding it—and it sees all these spirits as menacing and powerful. The target must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target believes every spirit it sees is an imminent threat. It’s [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 3 for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, but the effect lasts 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the target becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round. --- **Heightened (6th)** You can target up to 5 creatures.
name: Manifestation Of Spirits

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Pain Of Ages](/Spells.aspx?ID=2470)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=149" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 99

**Tradition**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Will

</row>

</column>

---

You sense a spiritual resonance of deep anguish in the earth beneath your feet, the walls surrounding you, or the fragments of souls left behind in a place of calamity. You draw on this reservoir of pain and unleash its force against your enemies, dealing 8d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. On a critical failure, the creature is also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) for 1 round as it reels from the impact of so much anguish.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d8.
name: Pain Of Ages

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Spirit Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=2471)
<actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 99

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** varies

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You draw on nearby spiritual energy or on echoes of the spirits you’ve invoked throughout your life to temporarily ward living flesh against dangerous spirits. You grant the target a +1 status bonus to saving throws against spells and effects caused by creatures that have the spirit trait and haunts. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters. 

<actions string="Single Action" /> The spell has a range of [touch](/Rules.aspx?ID=2238). 

<actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you target a living creature, the bonus increases to +2. 

<actions string="Three Actions" /> (concentrate) You create a ward in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). This targets you and all your allies in the burst.
name: Spirit Ward

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Spiritual Transport](/Spells.aspx?ID=2473)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 99

**Tradition**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 240 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You call upon the spiritual energies surrounding you to teleport you to an unoccupied space within range that you can see. Any items you’re wearing and holding come with you. Your familiar or animal companion, if you have one that’s sharing your space or adjacent to your space, is transported with you, but any other creature sharing your space remains in that space.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The range increases to 1 mile. You don’t need to be able to see your destination, as long as you have been there in the past and know its relative direction from you.
name: Spiritual Transport

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 99 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 120 feet **Area** 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) **Defense** Reflex **Duration** 1 minute --- You draw on the essence of spiritual energy surrounding you to manifest a misty veil. When a creature that isn’t [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to spirit damage begins its turn within the area, it must attempt a Reflex save. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the veil. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the beginning of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature is dazzled and gains a [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to spirit damage equal to half your level until the beginning of its next turn as spiritual energy clings to it. A creature with spiritsense can’t use that imprecise sense while it’s dazzled in this way.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the weakness is equal to your level.
name: Veil Of Spirits

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Elemental Sheath](/Spells.aspx?ID=2477)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 147

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You coat yourself in nature’s power. Choose [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508); the spell gains that trait. You become completely covered by armor formed from that element, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to two types of damage based on the element you choose. 

**Air** bludgeoning and [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586); **Earth** bludgeoning and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669); **Fire** fire and slashing; **Metal** acid and slashing; **Water** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) and piercing; **Wood** piercing and [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510).

---
**Heightened (+2)** The resistance increases by 5.
name: Elemental Sheath

[Friendly Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=2479)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 148

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You exert magical force to propel a willing creature up to 10 feet in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), including upward, though if they aren’t on solid ground or have another way to maintain their height (such as a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350)) when the movement ends, they fall. When you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell, you can move them again or choose a new target within range and move them instead.You can cast this spell on an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) ally, and if you do, the movement from this spell doesn’t trigger reactions.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The distance increases to 20 feet.

**Heightened (7th)** The distance increases to 30 feet.
name: Friendly Push

</row>

You know how to swiftly escape from harm. You instantly teleport out of danger into an unoccupied space within range, and you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to all damage against the triggering damage equal to your level. You can’t [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) if there’s no space to move into.

**Heightened (+2)** The range increases by 10 feet, and the damage increases by 3d8.

markdown:
**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 148 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Kyonin have access to this option._ **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** 10 minutes **Range** 1,000 miles **Target** you and up to 6 willing creatures touched **Duration** 1 hour --- You transport yourself and your allies to a peaceful extraplanar realm of towering trees, each of which holds a misty gateway to another location. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), you choose a specific destination within 1,000 miles that you can identify by its position relative to your starting position and by its appearance (or other identifying features). Incorrect knowledge of the location causes the spell to fail. You and your allies walk for 1 hour through the forest, and each of you regains Hit Points and reduces the value of any [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) or [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition as if you’d taken a [full night’s rest](/Rules.aspx?ID=2601). At the end of the journey, you attempt a DC 40 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) check to find the correct gate to your desired location. **Critical Success** You and your allies arrive within 1,000 feet of your desired location.
**Success** You and your allies arrive within 1 mile of your desired location.
**Failure** You and your allies arrive within 10 miles of your desired location.
**Critical Failure** You and your allies exit the forest within 1 mile of your original location.
name: Forest of Gates

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 149 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Kyonin have access to this option._ **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Area** 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) **Target** up to 2 creatures **Defense** Fortitude --- You wrench the targets off their feet, forcefully teleporting them within the area. The creatures must each be able to fit in their new space, and their positions must be unoccupied, entirely within the area, and in your line of sight. Each target attempts a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target can teleport if it wants, but it chooses the destination within the area.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** You teleport the target and choose its destination; the target takes 10d10 bludgeoning damage.
**Critical Failure** You teleport the target and choose its destination; the target takes 20d10 bludgeoning damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Sudden Transposition

[Trade Items](/Spells.aspx?ID=2485)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 149

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Kyonin have access to this option._

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 item you’re holding and 1 item in another creature’s possession you can see, each no more than light Bulk
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

You magically swap an item you’re holding for another that someone else has. The second item appears in the hand you were holding the first item (or it falls to the ground in your square if you’re unable to hold it), and the first item appears in the same place on the other creature’s possession where the second item was (held in a hand or attached to a belt, for instance). If the second item is in the possession of a creature unwilling to relinquish it, they can prevent the transposition with a successful Reflex save.

---
**Heightened (5th)** Each object’s maximum Bulk increases to 1. If one of the items is 1 Bulk and the other isn’t, an unwilling creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their saving throw.
name: Trade Items

---

You conjure a complex clockwork device on the ground and cause it to slowly spread across the battlefield. The area is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). Each creature that enters or ends its turn in the area takes 2d8 slashing damage with a basic Reflex save. A creature can take this damage only once per turn. On subsequent rounds, the first time you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell each round, you can expand the radius of the clockwork device by 5 feet.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d8.
name: Conjured Clockwork

May contain spoilers from Shades of Blood **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 81 **Duration** 1 minute --- You magically augment your body with clockwork parts and magitech innovations. Choose any one effect of your choice. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell.
  • **Ablative Plating** You coat your body with metal plates that protect your vital organs. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 1 to physical damage.
  • **Clockwork Arm** You transform one of your arms into a clockwork arm powered by gears, springs, and pneumatic actuators. You gain a clockwork fist [unarmed attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2191), which has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), and [free-hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613) traits and deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage.
  • **Spined Fingertips** Your fingers sprout microscopic metal spines that enable you to cling to surfaces like a [spider](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=474). You gain a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) of 20 feet.
  • **Spring-Loaded Legs** Your leg muscles are augmented with powerful springs. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed and double the distance you [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298).
--- **Heightened (3rd)** The ablative plating’s resistance increases to 2. The clockwork fist is a [_+1 striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon. **Heightened (5th)** The ablative plating’s resistance increases to 3, and the clockwork fist is a [_+2 striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon. **Heightened (7th)** The ablative plating’s resistance increases to 4, and the clockwork fist is a _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861)_ weapon. **Heightened (9th)** The ablative plating’s resistance increases to 5 and the clockwork fist is a [_+3 major striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon.
name: Augmented Body

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Shielding Formation](/Spells.aspx?ID=2489)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 69

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure magical shields of force to protect your allies around you. You and each ally who ends their turn within the emanation gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until they leave the emanation or the spell ends, whichever comes first. If an ally takes physical damage or damage from a spell or magical effect while being granted this bonus, they can choose to end the bonus for themselves as a free action to gain resistance 10 to all damage against the triggering damage. If they do, they become temporarily immune to the effects of shielding formation for 10 minutes. You can do the same by spending your reaction; if you do, you can’t cast shielding formation again for 10 minutes, though you can continue Sustaining the benefits for others.
name: Shielding Formation

<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

<row gap="medium">

Powerful feathered wings—usually vibrant white or jet black— emerge from your back, granting you a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) equal to your Speed. You can use these wings to attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) a creature; you don’t need a free hand to do so, and you can roll using your spell attack modifier instead of your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) skill for the check. When this spell’s duration ends, if you’re still flying, you float to the ground, as [_gentle landing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542).

---
**Heightened (5th)** The duration increases to 1 minute.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The amount of healing increases by 1d10.

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 84 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The poison inflicted by this spell has the incapacitation trait, but the spell itself does not. This means that a creature which fails the save against the spell begins at stage 1 for the poison._ **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 100 feet **Area** 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) **Defense** Fortitude --- You conjure an exploding glass container filled with a sightstealing poison and hurl it across enemy lines. Upon impact, the bottle bursts and exposes all creatures in the area to the toxin within. Each creature in the area must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 3d6 [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) damage.
**Failure** The creature is afflicted with blinding poison at stage 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is afflicted with blinding poison at stage 2. **Blinding Poison** ([incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Level** 9; **Maximum Duration** 4 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage and blinded for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 3** 5d6 poison damage and blinded for 1 round (1 round); **Stage 4** 6d6 poison damage and blinded for 1 minute (1 round)
name: Blinding Bottle

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Boots on the Ground](/Spells.aspx?ID=2495)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 84

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Will

**Duration** until you next make your daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You craft the illusion of your army being much bigger than it actually is, hoping to overwhelm the opposing forces and shatter their hopes. Your illusion can create up to three illusory Gargantuan duplicates of troops within range. You can give the illusion a basic task to perform, such as standing in formation, marching, mimicking guard movements, or unloading illusory boxes; however, the illusion can’t perform any actual tasks and can’t create intelligible sounds, such as music or clear speech. If you and any of the illusory troops are ever farther than 500 feet apart, the spell ends.

An illusory troop’s AC is equal to your spell DC. Its saving throws modifiers are equal to your spell DC – 10. If an illusory troop is hit by an attack or fails a save, you must attempt a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On success, the illusion of that troop is destroyed. On failure, the entire illusion shatters, and all illusory troops are destroyed.

---
**Heightened (8th)** The DC of the flat check is lowered to 8, and you can create up to five illusory troops.
name: Boots on the Ground

You toss a handful of magical cogs before you, summoning a battalion of clockwork soldiers to fight your enemies. The clockwork soldiers occupy the space of a Huge creature and have a Speed of 60 feet.

**Arrive** _Clockwork Assault_ Clockwork soldiers slash at your enemies with their halberds, dealing 4d10 slashing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to all enemy creatures in its location or within 15 feet.

**Depart** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) _Ticking Bomb_ The clockwork soldiers freeze abruptly before exploding into a mass of flying cogs and metal shrapnel, dealing 4d8 slashing damage and 4d8 fire damage in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature takes an additional 2d8 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 85 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Range** 500 feet **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- You tap into the glories of the armies of old, bringing forth their might to your battlefield and making their strength and power your own. This army manifests as glimmering forms of a conquering military of old, replete with banners and colors of their nation. The army occupies the space of a Gargantuan creature and has a Speed of 80 feet. **Arrive** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) _Banner’s Call_ The conquering army manifests before you and your allies, letting out a battle cry of victory and devotion. Each enemy within an 80-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2.
**Failure** The creature takes 6d12 mental damage and is frightened 3.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 12d12 mental damage, is frightened 4, and is [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round. **Depart** (emotion, [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), mental) _Trumpets of War_ As the conquering soldiers begin to fade, their military musicians play one more triumphant song in your honor. You and all allies within 100 feet of the soldiers immediately reduce the value of any [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), frightened, [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) conditions by 2 and gain a +2 status bonus to all attacks, saving throws, skill checks, and DCs for 3 rounds.
name: Conquering Soldiers

The triggering enemy attempts a Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The recoil from their ranged attack causes the target to be [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of their next turn.
**Failure** The recoil imposes a –1 status penalty to the ranged attack and renders the target off-guard until the beginning of their next turn.
**Critical Failure** The recoil imposes a –2 status penalty to the ranged attack and renders the target off-guard until the beginning of their next turn. Until the start of their next turn, any additional ranged attacks made with the same weapon, spell, or ability take the same penalty.
name: Curse of Recoil

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Dancing Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=2499)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 85

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 touched shield (unattended or in your possession) and 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You levitate the touched shield and send it to orbit around a creature. When you Cast the Spell and Sustain it, the shield uses the [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316) action to protect the target creature.

---
**Heightened (4th)** If you have the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction, you can use your reaction to have the target shield use Shield Block when the target creature would take damage from a physical attack, in addition to the usual trigger. In this case, Shield Block protects the target creature, rather than you. Since the shield is using Shield Block and not you, additional effects or abilities that normally apply when you Shield Block don’t apply.
name: Dancing Shield

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Desperate Repair](/Spells.aspx?ID=2500)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** A shield within range would be reduced to 0 Hit Points by a damaging effect

**Requirements** The triggering shield had more than 1 Hit Point before taking damage.
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 shield
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You hold a shield together with the power of your magic, forcing wood and steel to fuse back together. The shield remains at 1 Hit Point and isn’t [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46). Its construction is weakened until someone takes the time to repair it, preventing desperate repair or a similar ability from saving the same shield until your next daily preparations.
name: Desperate Repair
requirement_markdown: The triggering shield had more than 1 Hit Point before taking damage.

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Dividing Trench](/Spells.aspx?ID=2501)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You dig an earthen trench across the surrounding terrain, which must be soil, clay, sand, or other soft earth, but not flooring or stone. The trench is 5 feet wide, 5 feet deep, and up to 15 feet long. You can shape the trench’s path, but it must be one continuous line. You must conjure the trench in an open space so it doesn’t pass through any creatures or objects. Creatures inside the trench have cover from creatures outside the trench, and creatures outside the trench have [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) from creatures inside the trench. Small or smaller creatures in the trench might have greater cover against other creatures who aren’t close to the trench’s edge, and in turn, those creatures might have more cover against small creatures in the trench; the GM determines the amount of additional cover, if any. Entering or leaving the trench requires an additional 5 feet of movement but doesn’t require any kind of check and isn’t considered [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). At the end of the spell’s duration, the trench disappears, and all creatures in the trench remain in their spaces on solid ground.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The trench’s maximum length increases by 15 feet.
name: Dividing Trench

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Explosive Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=2502)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

**Area** 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) plus additional 5-foot bursts

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) fortitude

</row>

</column>

---

You fire a booming, fiery explosion followed by a salvo of smaller blasts, each cracking the air with the sound of thunder. You create a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) and 1d4 additional 5-foot bursts each within the range and within 20 feet of another burst. None of the bursts can intersect. Each creature caught in at least one of these bursts takes 6d8 fire damage and 6d4 sonic damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failed save, a creature is also deafened for 1 minute.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The fire damage increases by 1d8 and the sonic damage increases by 1d4.
name: Explosive Barrage

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Fallen Soldier's Lament](/Spells.aspx?ID=2503)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

**Target** 1 corpse of a Medium or smaller creature that has died within the past 8 hours
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Will

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You raise an illusion in the space of a fallen foe, crafting it to resemble a ghost of your target before parading it across the battlefield. Whenever you [Cast this Spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) or [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) this spell, you move the illusion up to 30 feet and cause each enemy in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of the spirit to attempt a Will save against your spell DC. Enemies who fail become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (or frightened 2 on a critical failure).

---
**Heightened (6th)** You can target a corpse of any size that has died within the past 8 hours.
name: Fallen Soldier's Lament

[Filter Air](/Spells.aspx?ID=2504)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Subtle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=522" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You’re exposed to a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) that has the [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) trait, and you’re aware of the exposure

**Requirements** You breathe air.
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You suck in a rough breath of air, and your body automatically filters out the harmful molecules that would infest your lungs. As you breathe for the duration of the spell, you get the outcome one degree of success higher on your saving throws against inhaled poisons and diseases.
name: Filter Air

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 86 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You soften the target’s blows, ensuring they avoid vital areas and cause no lasting harm. **Failure** The creature is affected for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature is affected for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (4th)** The range increases to 100 feet, and you can target up to 8 creatures.

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Frozen Fog](/Spells.aspx?ID=2506)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) fortitude

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure a chilling mist that settles into the bones and makes it difficult to move. This has the effects of [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), except that you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) the spell once per round to move the fog up to 10 feet. A creature that starts its turn in the area takes 6d8 cold damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 1 minute (clumsy 2 on a critical failure) and takes a –10-foot status penalty to Speeds for as long as it’s clumsy.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 3d8.
name: Frozen Fog

[Helpful Reload](/Spells.aspx?ID=2507)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 87

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** One of your allies makes a ranged Strike with a weapon with a reload of 1 or less

**Requirements** Your ally has more ammunition for their ranged weapon available.
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** the triggering creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

With a quick gesture, you reload your ally’s weapon, allowing them to focus on fighting the enemy. You reload their weapon with a piece of mundane ammunition from the triggering creature’s inventory.

---
**Heightened (4th)** You reload your ally’s weapon and grant them a +1 status bonus to their next Strike with this weapon.

**Heightened (6th)** You reload your ally’s weapon with the ammunition of their choice from their inventory. Your ally gains a +1 status bonus to their next Strike with this weapon.

**Heightened (8th)** The spell no longer requires your ally to have appropriate ammunition available. You summon a single piece of common magical ammunition of 10th level or lower to reload your ally’s weapon with. Your ally gains a +1 status bonus to their next Strike with this weapon.
name: Helpful Reload
requirement_markdown: Your ally has more ammunition for their ranged weapon available.

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 87 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 100 feet **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- In stories, armies with just and righteous causes win with little difficulty, conquering unholy legions. However, many holy armies know that an extra hand never hurts in the fight to exterminate cruelty and unholy forces, and they might call upon the assistance of a holy army. The holy army occupies the space of a Huge creature. When you Cast this Spell, choose one of the holy militaries below to summon.
  • **Agathions** Speed 30 feet; **Arrive** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) _Pranks for All_ A squadron of [procyals](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1057) arrives, eager to see your enemies learn their lesson in this fight. All enemies in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) take 8d6 fire damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) as the procyals bombard them with popping firecrackers; **Depart** _Parting Lesson_ ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The procyals laugh uproariously as they leave, each throwing out a piece of unrelated advice to a nearby enemy. Enemies in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387)take 4d6 mental damage (basic Will save). On a failure, a creature is also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. On a critical failure, they're stunned 2.
  • **Angels** Speed 30 feet, fly 40 feet; **Arrive** ([spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) _FlamingTruth_ A legion of [balisses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2816) arrive in a burst of holy flame, dealing 8d6 spirit damage to all creatures in a 20-foot emanation with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, a creature is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round; **Depart** _Hidden Departure_ With a plume of smoke, the balisses vanish, spreading a thick cloud of smoke in a 10-foot emanation. All creatures within the smoke are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures within the smoke are [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2, and [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creatures within the smoke gain the effects of [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) and ignore the concealment granted by the smoke.
  • **Archons** Speed 30 feet, fly 60 feet; **Arrive** ([spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) _Justice Prevails_ battalion of [aesras](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2834), also known as legion archons, swoop in to provide assistance. Enemies within a 60-foot cone take 8d6 spirit damage (basic Reflex save); **Depart** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) _Eyes of Judgment_ With one final examination of the battlefield, the aesras take their leave, but not before bringing down their fiery swords to create a corridor of sacred flame. Enemies in a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) take 4d6 slashing damage and 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (basic Reflex save).
  • **Azatas** Speed 30 feet, fly 80 feet; **Arrive** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) _Inspiring Song_ [Kanya muses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2842) arrive to embolden your allies while pushing back your foes. Enemies in a 20-foot emanation take 6d6 sonic damage (basic Fortitude save). A creature that fails the save is also pushed 10 feet away from the kanyas. Allies in the emanation gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws, as well as +4 status bonus to damage rolls for the duration of the spell; **Depart** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) _Booming Finale_ With a thunderous chord that stirs the hearts of your forces, the kanyas depart. Enemies in a 60-foot cone must attempt a Will save. On a failure, they're [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2, and on a critical failure, they're frightened 3 and [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round. Allies in this cone regain 4d10+16 Hit Points.
  • **Garudas** Speed 25 feet, fly 60 feet; **Arrive** _Swooping Dive_ A battalion of [garudas](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1162) sweep through enemy lines, dealing 6d10 slashing damage to enemies in a 120-foot line (basic Reflex save); **Depart** _Winds of Freedom_ The garudas flap their wings in tandem, focusing on a 20-foot burst within 60 feet of them. All allies in that area gain a +1 circumstance bonus to all skill checks and DCs for 1 round, and enemies must succeed a Reflex save or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Holy Host

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Instant Minefield](/Spells.aspx?ID=2509)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Subtle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=522" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 88

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You create several hidden mines throughout the area that explode when stepped on. Choose up to 6 unoccupied squares within range. A mine appears in each chosen square, visible only to you and your allies. A mine can’t be targeted and isn’t affected by area effects, but if it hasn’t been triggered by the end of the spell’s duration, it dissipates harmlessly. A creature that can see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) creatures and objects can see the mines. A creature can also attempt a Perception check against your spell DC to Seek to find an invisible mine.

A creature who enters a square with an invisible mine triggers it, causing an explosion that deals 3d6 fire damage and 3d6 piercing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to the creature and every creature in a 5-foot emanation from the mine’s square.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The fire damage and piercing damage dealt by a mine each increase by 1d6.
name: Instant Minefield
