<title level="1" right="Spell 10" pfs="Standard">
[Jassim's Allegiance](/Spells.aspx?ID=2510)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Incarnate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=388" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 88

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

You bartered for this spell in some strange market or perhaps gained it while wandering the desert, hopelessly lost and surrounded by nothing but the remnants of time and fallen empires. With a gesture and words of a language not spoken since the fall of the Hanging City of Teskra, you summon Jassim the Wanderer. Jassim, a true Jistkan automaton, occupies the space of a Gargantuan creature and has a Speed of 60 feet. 

**Arrive** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) _Eyes of Fire_ Jassim emerges from the ground and glares at your enemies, a fearsome look forming on a face of ancient metal and glass. He shoots an energy beam in a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 5d12 fire damage and 5d12 piercing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Enemies that critically fail are [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2. 

**Depart** ([metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) _Heart of Rust_ Jassim takes a metal piece from his chest and holds it out, crushing it in his fist and allowing the powder to spread across a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). These particles deal 10d6 slashing damage to all creatures and unattended objects within the area (basic Fortitude save). A metal creature that fails its save also takes 2d8 [persistent slashing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). A non-metal creature that fails this save is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2.
name: Jassim's Allegiance

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 88 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 30 feet **Target** one object held by one creature and the creature holding it **Defense** Reflex **Duration** varies --- One object held by a creature becomes fused to that creature’s hand and can’t be put down. To [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) the item, the result of the skill check must exceed the spell’s DC or the normal DC, whichever is higher. To voluntarily drop the weapon, the target must spend an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action rather than a free action, and then succeed at a Reflex save; the target must also attempt a Reflex save to Interact to put away or swap the object. On a failed save, the action is wasted, but on a success, they drop the item and the spell ends. An unwilling creature must attempt an initial Reflex save against _lock item_. A willing creature can choose to critically fail the saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is affected for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is affected for 4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature is affected for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (+2)** You can target either one additional object held by the same creature or one additional object held by one additional creature.
name: Lock Item

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 89 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 30 feet **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- Sometimes the most common irritations can band together and become threats to your enemies. You reach out to the pests and nuisances around you, forming a small squadron of them to harass and occupy your opponents. They swarm from the forests, the air, and even the dirt beneath your feet. The pest squadron occupies the space of a Large creature, and like a swarm, they can occupy the same space as other creatures. When you Cast this Spell, choose one of the pest squadrons below to summon.
  • **Cockroaches** Speed 30 feet, climb 30 feet; **Arrive** _Scurrying Swarm_ The swarm of cockroaches scuttles toward the enemy, swarming them and climbing up the bodies of any enemy touching the ground. They deal 4d6 piercing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to all enemies within their space and in a 5-foot emanation. On a critical failure, a creature can't use concentrate actions for 1 round as they try and brush away the insects; **Depart** _Farewell Dirt_ The cockroaches disperse, climbing down from the creatures and leaving behind their debris and dirt. All enemies in their space must attempt a Fortitude save. On a failure, a creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 (sickened 3 on a critical failure).
  • **Mosquitoes** Speed 30 feet, fly 50 feet; **Arrive** _Biting Flight_ The mosquitoes arrive in a hazy cloud, landing on the exposed skin of your opponents and dealing 4d6 piercing damage to enemies in their space (basic Reflex save); **Depart** _Parting Gift_ The swarm attempts to drain their opponents before flying off. All enemies in their space and in a 5-foot emanation must succeed at a Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (drained 2 on a critical failure).
  • **Pigeons** Speed 20 feet, fly 40 feet; **Arrive** _Dive Bomb_ The swarm of pigeons dives into the crowd of enemies and attacks with beaks and talons. Each enemy within their space takes 2d8 piercing and 2d4 slashing damage (basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round; **Depart** ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) _Wing Snap_ The pigeon swarm snaps their wingtips together to startle their opponents before leaving. Each enemy within their space takes 4d6 sonic damage (basic Fortitude save).
  • **Rats** Speed 30 feet, burrow 20 feet; **Arrive** _Chittering Race_ Rats swarm the legs of your enemies, biting at them and digging their sharp nails into whatever exposed flesh reveals itself. Each enemy in their space takes 4d6 piercing damage (basic Reflex save); **Depart** _Underfoot_ The rats scurry underfoot, tripping up enemies and burrowing underground to make a swift getaway. Each enemy in their space must succeed a Reflex save or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and take 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The area the rat swarm was in becomes difficult terrain for 1 round.
  • **Skunks** Speed 30 feet, climb 20 feet; **Arrive** _Mustelid Charge_ Skunks race to the scene, and each enemy within their space and within a 5-foot emanation take 4d6 piercing damage (basic Reflex save) as the skunks snarl and bite; **Depart** (acid) _Stink Bomb_ Before leaving, the skunks launch a final offensive maneuver and release their stink glands in a 15-foot cone. All creatures in the area take 2d6 acid damage (basic Fortitude save). On a failure, a creature is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure).
  • **Spiders** Speed 30 feet, climb 30 feet; **Arrive** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) _Venomous Swarm_ horde of violin spiders crawls up from the ground, climbing onto enemies within their space and in a 5-foot emanation and biting them. Each enemy takes 3d6 piercing damage (basic Fortitude save). Enemies who fail are exposed to violin spider venom; **Depart** _Spinning Webs_ As the violin spiders depart, they spray sticky webs at their enemies in a 30-foot cone. All creatures in this cone must attempt a Reflex save. On a failure, a creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds (–20-foot circumstance penalty on a critical failure) for 1 minute; a creature can remove this penalty with a successful [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) against your spell DC. The area of the cone is also difficult terrain for 1 round.
    **Violin Spider Venom** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison, 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); Stage 4 2d6 poison, 2d6 persistent bleed, and clumsy 1 (1 round)
name: Pest Swarm

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Siege Weapon's Blessing](/Spells.aspx?ID=2514)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 89

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** one mounted siege weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You trace a magic rune onto the body of a siege weapon, calling upon fate or your faith to guide the attacks your allies make with this machine. For the spell’s duration, any skill checks attempted to Load the siege weapon can be rolled twice, taking the better result; these skill checks gain the [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) trait. In addition, any saving throws attempted by creatures caught in the siege weapon’s Launch area must be rolled twice, taking the worse result; these saving throws gain the [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) trait.

---
**Heightened (9th)** The duration is 8 hours.
name: Siege Weapon's Blessing

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Skeleton Army](/Spells.aspx?ID=2515)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Incarnate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=388" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 90

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

With a fistful of compressed bones, you summon a legion of [skeletal hulks](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3197) to fight in your war, refusing to let them rest until they’ve given up their last inches of usefulness to your cause. The undead army occupies the space of a Huge creature and has a Speed of 40 feet.

**Arrive** _Bone March_ The skeletal hulks rise from the dirt, slashing through your enemies and attacking with their dirt-encrusted, bony hands. Enemies within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 4d8 slashing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature also take 2d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). 

**Depart** _Tossed Heads_ Before crumpling into a pile of bones, the skeleton army removes their skulls and throws them at the enemy lines, chattering and howling, dealing 6d6 piercing damage to enemies in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (basic Reflex save) within 30 feet. On a critical failure, a creature is also [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2.
name: Skeleton Army

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Steel Fortifications](/Spells.aspx?ID=2516)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 90

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure several metal structures that consist of angled beams attached to one another at a central point, which can help block attacks. You create up to three Large structures within range in unoccupied squares; each fortification is 8 feet long, 8 feet wide, and 8 feet tall. There must be enough room for a fortification in order to conjure it. A single fortification has an AC of 10 and is immune to critical hits and precision damage. It has [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 9, 60 Hit Points, and a BT of 30. Once a fortification is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), it crumbles into sharp metal bits. Any creature adjacent to or sharing a space with a fortification when it is broken must attempt a Reflex save or take 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). 

Creatures can pass through the spaces of a fortification, though Medium and larger creatures treat these spaces as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). A Small or smaller creature can occupy the same spaces as a fortification; doing so grants them standard cover. Similarly, a [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) Medium creature can occupy the same spaces as a fortification, gaining standard cover in the process. A Medium or smaller creature can climb a fortification with a successful DC 15 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check.
name: Steel Fortifications

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 91 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 100 feet **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- Reaching out to the legions of the wicked, you call upon them for assistance. They emerge, bubbling up from the ground, and stand to face your enemies, malevolent and ready for bloodshed. The unholy army occupies the space of a Huge creature. When you Cast this Spell, choose one of the unholy squadrons below to summon.
  • **Asuras** Speed 40 feet; **Arrive** _Dance of Duality_ A legion of [adhukaits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1070) arrive on the field, dancing and scurrying in and among your enemy's front line. All enemies in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 10d6 slashing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) as the adhukaits slash at them with their kukris; **Depart** _Split Remembrance_ As the adhukaits begin to retreat, they argue about who did the most damage and decide to see which half is stronger. All enemies in a 20-foot emanation take 6d6 bludgeoning damage (basic Fortitude save) as they're violently yanked by adhukaits. On a failure, a creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 1 minute (clumsy 2 on a critical failure).
  • **Daemons** Speed 25 feet, fly 40 feet; **Arrive** (disease) _Winds of Pestilence _ [Leukodaemons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2893) fly into position, their wings beating with the dust of a thousand diseases of a thousand years. Enemies in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) take 8d8 piercing damage (basic Fortitude save). On a failure, a creature is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure); **Depart** (disease) _Remember Me_ The leukodaemons claw at your enemies with their infected nails. All enemies in a 10-foot emanation take 6d6 slashing damage (basic Reflex save). On a failure, a creature is also drained 1 (drained 2 on a critical failure).
  • **Demons** Speed 25 feet; **Arrive** (fire) _Fuming Entrance_ A legion of [brimoraks](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2896) arrive in clouds of thick black smoke. The army's space and every square in a 20-foot emanation is choked with burning ash and smog, making all creatures outside the smoke [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to creatures within it and all creatures within the smoke concealed to creatures outside the smoke and other creatures within it. This smoke lasts for 1 minute, and a creature who enters or begins its turn within the smoke must attempt a Fortitude save or take 2d6 persistent fire damage and become sickened 1 (3d6 persistent fire damage and sickened 2 on a critical failure); **Depart** (fire) _Swords of Flame_ The arson demons swing their flaming swords through your enemies' ranks. All enemies in a 10-foot emanation take 4d8 slashing damage and 3d6 fire damage (basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature also takes 2d8 persistent fire damage.
  • **Devils** Speed 25 feet; **Arrive** ([spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) _Diabolic Infantry_ A legion of vordines swarm the battlefield. All enemies in a 20-foot emanation take 4d10 piercing damage and 2d6 spirit damage (basic Reflex save) as the vordines impale them with their tridents; **Depart** ([nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)) _Whipped and Tripped_ The devils' whips lengthen impossibly to flense flesh from soul. Enemies in two non-intersecting 40-foot lines take 6d8 nonlethal bludgeoning damage (basic Reflex save). On a failure, a creature is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). On a critical failure, it's also knocked prone and [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round.
  • **Divs** Speed 35 feet; **Arrive** (mental) _Deceptive Delight_ A battalion of sepids arrives to torment your enemies. Enemies in a 60-foot cone take 6d6 mental damage (basic Will save). On a failure, a creature is confused for 1 round; **Depart** (spirit) Battle Debris The sepids slam their falchions into the remnants of the chaos of battle, shattering bones, which explode out in a 30-foot emanation. All non-fiends in the area take 8d6 slashing damage and 3d6 spirit damage from debris (basic Reflex save).
  • **Qlippoths** Speed 40 feet, climb 40 feet, fly 40 feet; **Arrive** ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) _Terrifying Scuttle_ A horde of [gongorinans](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3155) opens their maws to reveal the horror inside. All enemies in a 60-foot cone must succeed at a Will save or be sickened 2 (sickened 2 and blinded for 1 round on a critical failure); **Depart** _Pincer Assault_ The gongorinans lash out at everyone. All enemies in a 20-foot emanation take 6d12 slashing damage (basic Reflex save).
  • **Velstracs** Speed 25 feet; **Arrive** _Chains of Pain_ A legion of [sacristans](/Monsters.aspx?ID=850) emerges from the shadows, their barbed chains wrapping around enemy limbs to prevent all escape. Enemies in a 20-foot emanation must attempt a Reflex save. On a failure, they take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds and 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). On a critical failure they take a –20-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds and 4d6 persistent bleed damage. This penalty to Speeds lasts until they recover from their persistent bleed damage; **Depart** _Pleasant Screams_ The sacristans rip their chains from their victims, screaming in unison with their pain. Any enemies who failed the initial Reflex save during the sacristans' arrival take 4d8 piercing damage (basic Fortitude save). In addition, everyone in a 60-foot cone must attempt a Will save. On a failure, they're frightened 2 and deafened for 1 minute (frightened 3 and deafened for 1 minute on a critical failure); this effect has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits.
name: Unholy Army

**Target** one  club  or  staff  you're wielding

<row gap="medium">

You transform a bludgeon into a fearsome torch suited to casting [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) into the afterlife. One end of the weapon ignites, becoming wreathed in blue flame. While you wield the target weapon, it becomes a +1 weapon, gains the [versatile fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) trait, and sheds light as brightly as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760). Whenever you successfully Strike a creature with the weapon, you can Dismiss the spell as a free action to deal 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the target; if the target is undead, increase the persistent damage dice to d8s.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The persistent fire damage increases by 1d6.

[Armor of Thorn and Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=2524)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
</traits>

](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 224

[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Razor-sharp thorns and claws erupt from your skin or scales. Whenever a creature touches you or hits you with a melee unarmed attack, it takes 1 piercing damage. Additionally, if you become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), or otherwise held [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in a creature’s grasp, such as by being engulfed or swallowed, the creature takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

---
**Heightened (+2)** The piercing damage increases by 2, and the persistent bleed damage increases by 1d4.
name: Armor of Thorn and Claw

<actions string="Three Actions" />

<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />

Like a vorpal dragon, you absorb life with an inhale and release it with an exhale. All living creatures in the first cone take 5d6 void damage with a basic Fortitude save. **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 224 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will --- You channel your heightened sense of self-worth into a creature, forcing them to acknowledge your true worth. You can [Cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) this Spell using 1 action if you know the target isn’t worthy of you spending your time to generate the full force of this spell, or using 3 actions if you can’t possibly hold back the majesty of your presence and must flood the target with your full attention. The target takes 5d10 mental damage and other effects depending on its Will saving throw. If you Cast the Spell using 1 action, the target gets a result one degree of success better than it rolled. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage and is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with you for 1 round.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with you for 1 round. While fascinated with you, the target must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) when attempting to [Cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) a Spell or use an action with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait. On a failure, the action is disrupted as the target blurts praise for you instead of saying what it intended.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but double damage and the effect lasts for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d10.
name: Channel Arrogance

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Cloud Current](/Spells.aspx?ID=2527)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 224

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

Air and water vapor combine into a great cloud that funnels a powerful current within it. The eddies make the cloud appear from a distance to be a group of [cloud dragons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=625) tumbling through the sky. The cloud is up to 30 feet wide, 30 feet tall, and 10 miles long. It runs parallel to the ground, and the nearest end of it must be within range and at least 100 feet above the ground. The current travels in a direction you choose when you [Cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) the Spell, and the [travel Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2581) of creatures flying through the cloud in that direction is doubled. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the cloud.

---
**Heightened (5th)** The duration is 8 hours, and the cloud can be up to 50 feet wide and 100 miles long.

**Heightened (8th)** As 6th, and travel speed is quadrupled.
name: Cloud Current

[Divine Dragon's Watch](/Spells.aspx?ID=2528)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Contingency" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=385" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Sanctified" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=519" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=149" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 224

**Tradition**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 24 hours
</row>

</column>

---

You summon an invisible divine draconic spirit to watch over your target. When the spell is complete, you gain the Dragon’s Protection reaction; once you use the reaction, the spell ends. 

**Dragon’s Protection** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Trigger** An enemy you can see would reduce this spell’s target to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The dragon intercepts, preventing the spell’s target from being knocked out; the spell’s target remains at 1 Hit Point. As the dragon disappears, it flies over the triggering enemy and releases its retributive breath, dealing 5d4 spirit damage to it with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4.
name: Divine Dragon's Watch

<title level="1" right="Spell 10" pfs="Standard">
[Dragon Turret](/Spells.aspx?ID=2529)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 225

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500 feet

**Area** an empty 10-foot radius, 50-foot-tall cylinder

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained for up to 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure forth a massive tower with a large dragon that’s bound to crawl its length. The dragon and the tower share the same space, and the dragon intercepts any attack targeting the tower. When the dragon dies, the tower crumbles. The dragon is immune to all conditions, has an AC of 40, a +30 modifier for all saving throws, and 250 Hit Points. When you Cast the Spell, and once per round when you Sustain it, the dragon unleashes its breath in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), dealing 13d8 damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage type depends on the tradition used to cast this spell: **[arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)** [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), **[divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)** [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), **[occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), **[primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604).
name: Dragon Turret

**Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 225 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Damage is too high for a spell of its rank._ **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Area** 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) **Defense** Fortitude --- You erupt into the air with the force of a dragon taking flight, launching nearby creatures into the sky with you. This spell has no effect unless you’re on solid ground when you cast it. You fly directly upward, up to 100 feet into the air. When the spell ends, you safely float to the ground, as [_gentle landing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542). Every other creature in the area when you [Cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) this Spell must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is launched the same distance into the air as you, then falls.
**Failure** The creature is launched the same distance into the air as you, then falls and can’t [Arrest its Fall](/Actions.aspx?ID=2308).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes twice as much damage from falling.
name: Earth and Sky

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Horde of Underlings](/Spells.aspx?ID=2531)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 225

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You summon six underlings, such as [skeletons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=472) or [kobolds](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=435). When you cast this spell, you decide what kind of creatures they are, if they’re Small or Medium, and whether they deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (choose once and applying it to all underlings). Each underling has 1 Hit Point, 5 AC, and automatically fails all saving throws. Each underling appears in an unoccupied square of your choice within the area. The underlings don’t block movement, but they are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586) for creatures other than you.

Each underling attacks one enemy adjacent to it (if any), automatically dealing 1d4 damage of the chosen type. The First time you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell on each subsequent round, each underling moves 20 feet toward the nearest enemy. If an underling ends its movement adjacent to an enemy, it damages that enemy as described above.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The number of underlings increases by 2.
name: Horde of Underlings

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Limited">
[Lashunta's Life Bubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=2533)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 8 hours
</row>

</column>

---

You create a replenishing protective bubble of fresh, temperate air around the target. The shell of air enables the target to breathe even underwater or in a vacuum, protects from severe cold and heat, and grants a +1 status bonus to the target’s saves against inhaled dangers such as inhaled poisons and [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722).

---
**Heightened (6th)** The bubble lasts until your next preparation.

**Heightened (8th)** The bubble lasts until your next preparation, protects against extreme cold and heat, and grants a +2 status bonus instead of +1.
name: Lashunta's Life Bubble

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 233 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** touch **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude --- Your touch afflicts the target with an advanced form of scurvy, a mariner’s disease stemming from improper nutrition. The symptoms of scurvy include fatigue, joint pain, loose teeth, and internal hemorrhaging. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute.
**Failure** The target is afflicted with advanced scurvy at stage 1.
**Critical Failure** The target is afflicted with advanced scurvy at stage 2. **Advanced Scurvy** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Level** 4; for 1 day after eating fresh fruit, a creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their next saving throw against this affliction; **Stage 1** enfeebled 1 and the damage dealt by [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) effects is increased by 1 (1 day); **Stage 2** enfeebled 1, [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and the damage dealt by persistent bleed effects is increased by 1d4 (2 days); **Stage 3** enfeebled 2, fatigued, and the damage dealt by persistent bleed effects is increased by 1d6 (4 days)
name: Advanced Scurvy

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Limited">
[Lashing Rope](/Spells.aspx?ID=2536)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 234

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 50 feet

**Target** up to 50 feet of unattended  rope  or an inanimate rope-like object
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You cause a section of rope or a rope-like object to animate, slither to your square, and encircle you, wreathing you in twisting, lashing fibers.

When you Cast the Spell and each time you Sustain the Spell, you can make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with the rope, which uses and contributes to your multiple attack penalty. These rope Strikes are melee spell attacks; have the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), and [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716) weapon traits; and deal 3d6 slashing damage.

If you critically fail a check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) using the rope, you can’t make any more Strikes with it this turn, nor can you use it to make a [Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256) until the beginning of your next turn. This replaces the usual results of a critical failure to Trip.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage from your rope Strikes increases by 2d6.
name: Lashing Rope

The target must attempt a Will saving throw, with the following results. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The next time the target takes a [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) action, the target takes 1d4 mental damage; the spell’s duration then ends.
**Failure** The first time in a round when the target takes a hostile action, the target takes 1d4 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** Each time the target takes a hostile action, the target takes 1d4 mental damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The mental damage increases by 1d4.
name: Cycle of Retribution

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 287 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters must have the appropriate Season of Ghosts boon to access this option._ **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 living creature **Defense** Will **Duration** 1 minute --- You draw upon the target’s negative fate, causing this lingering destiny to afflict it and its nearby allies with the unintended consequences of its past lives. Spiritual echoes of the target’s past lives instill overwhelming feelings of doubt, regret, and despair into the minds of the target and its nearby allies. The effects depend on the target’s Will save, with the following results. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 4d6 mental damage, and then an aura of shimmering mist with a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) surrounds the creature. This mist isn’t dense enough to affect sight, but it does cause the creature and any of its allies in the aura to take a –1 status penalty to all skill checks.
**Failure** As success, but the creature takes an initial 8d6 mental damage and is then [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for the duration of the effect.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature takes an initial 16d6 mental damage, then is slowed 1 for the duration of the effect. Any of the target’s allies who begin their turn in the 10-foot emanation is slowed 1 for 1 round.
name: Fateful Condemnation

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Limited">
[Fated Healing](/Spells.aspx?ID=2539)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 287

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters must have the appropriate Season of Ghosts boon to access this option._

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** creatures who are  hostile  toward each other
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 5 rounds
</row>

</column>

---

You speak about the consequences of actions people take against each other and how it’s possible to break cycles of violence simply by making a different choice. The targets regain 1d4 Hit Points at the end of each of their own turns while the spell is in effect. If a target uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against the other target, the spell ends for the target that used the hostile action.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The targets regain an additional 1d4 Hit Points at the end of their own turns.
name: Fated Healing

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 287 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters must have the appropriate Season of Ghosts boon to access this option._ **Tradition** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 120 feet **Defense** Will **Duration** 1 minute --- You create a flowing river of ghostly water and that sheds [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for 10 feet on each side. The river begins in an adjacent square to you and extends in a straight 5-foot path to its maximum range or until it hits a solid barrier, whichever comes first. A creature that begins its turn in the glowing waters of the river or who enters one of the river’s spaces must attempt a Will save. If the creature is [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) or a [nindoru](/Traits.aspx?ID=739), it takes 4d6 mental damage, modified by the result of their Will save. **Critical Success** The creature ignores the river’s effects until the start of its next turn. If the creature is [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) or a [nindoru](/Traits.aspx?ID=739), it takes no damage.
**Success** The creature treats all squares occupied by the river as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586) until the start of its next turn. If the creature is undead or a nindoru, it takes half damage.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). If the creature is undead or a nindoru, it takes full damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is pushed 20 feet along the river’s path in the direction of flow. If the creature is undead or a nindoru, it takes double mental damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The mental damage increases by 1d6.
name: Life's Flowing River

Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature’s fate becomes distorted, and momentary conflicting glimpses of the immediate future cause the creature to become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is off-guard for the duration of the spell. If more than one of the targets failed to resist this spell, the creature also becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for the duration of the spell as its fate and those of the other creatures continue to clash and strain against each other.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but any creature that becomes stupefied 1 for the duration of the spell also becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for the spell’s duration.
name: Entangle Fate

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Limited">
[Life's Fresh Bloom](/Spells.aspx?ID=2542)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 308

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 5 rounds
</row>

</column>

---

The ground around you blooms with life, sprouting sparkling grass, flowers, and small shrubs. Each living and non-[nindoru](/Traits.aspx?ID=739) creature that starts its turn standing on the ground in the area recovers 1d6 Hit Points and gains a +5‑foot status bonus to its Speed until the end of its next turn. Nindoru fiends and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) that start their turn in the area of life’s fresh bloom instead become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 unless they succeed at a Fortitude saving throw.

---
**Heightened (7th)** Increase the Hit Points restored to 1d8 and the sickened condition value to 2.

**Heightened (10th)** Increase the Hit Points restored to 1d10 and the sickened condition value to 3.
name: Life's Fresh Bloom

[Prophet's Luck](/Spells.aspx?ID=2543)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 309

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You or a creature within range attempts a saving throw against an obvious threat

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You prophesize the result of the triggering saving throw as either a success (including a critical success) or a failure (including a critical failure). The target gains a +1 status bonus to the save if you predict success or a –1 status penalty if you predict failure. If you predict the result correctly, fortune favors you, and you gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws. If you predict incorrectly, fortune spurns the target, imparting a –1 status penalty to its saving throws.

---
**Heightened (6th)** The save bonus or penalty increases to +2 or –2.

**Heightened (9th)** The save bonus or penalty increases to +3 or –3.
name: Prophet's Luck

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) _Sacred nimbus_ deals 5d6 spirit damage with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait._

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 10-foot square

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You summon a small, fluffy white nimbus cloud that floats 10 feet above the ground and rains down pleasantly warm sanctified water. The rain deals 5d6 spirit damage to [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and creatures that have a [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to spirit damage in the area at the end of your turn. When you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell, you can move the cloud at a [fly speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 15 feet to anywhere in the spell’s range. The water dissipates as it makes contact with any creatures or surfaces, leaving only a bit of humidity in its place.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Sacred Nimbus

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Limited">
[Wall of Mirrors](/Spells.aspx?ID=2546)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 309

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You create a solid wall of shimmering reflective glass up to 50 feet long and 20 feet high. The wall is an inch thick and stands vertically. You must create the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don’t pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. Each 10-foot-by- 10-foot section of the wall has AC 10, [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 10, and 40 Hit Points, and it’s [immune to critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314) and precision damage. Everything on each side of the wall is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from creatures on the opposite side. If a creature Strikes the wall, the wall causes the creature to endure the mental anguish of that attack’s pain as well; the creature takes 4d4 mental damage and can resist this damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The Hit Points of each section of the wall increase by 10.
name: Wall of Mirrors

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Telekinetic Rend](/Spells.aspx?ID=2547)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 17

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** 2 non-overlapping 5-foot bursts

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude

</row>

</column>

---

Your mind creates a violent axis of motion in a nearby space that can smash or sever at your will. You deal your choice of 1d6 bludgeoning or 1d6 slashing damage to all creatures in the area, with a basic Fortitude save. A creature large enough to be in more than one of the bursts takes damage only once.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** Your thoughts expand in scope and power. The bursts deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage and 1d6 slashing damage, instead of the usual damage. A creature that critically fails its save is also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.

**Amp Heightened (+2)**
Both types of damage increase by 1d6 instead of just one. Add a third non-overlapping 5-foot burst to the area.
</column>
name: Telekinetic Rend

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Cantrip 3" pfs="Standard">
[Vector Screen](/Spells.aspx?ID=2548)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 17

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** a 10-foot line

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You set up a transparent, rippling screen of telekinetic energy that seizes small, fast-moving projectiles. Creatures can pass through the screen, but ammunition from physical ranged attacks—such as arrows, bolts, sling bullets, and other objects of similar size—is automatically trapped in the screen, clattering to the ground once the spell ends. Attacks with bigger ranged weapons, such as javelins, take a –2 circumstance penalty to their attack rolls if their paths pass through the screen. Massive ranged weapons and spell effects that don't create physical objects pass through the screen with no penalty.

---
**Heightened (5th)** The screen is 20 feet long.

**Heightened (6th)** The screen is 20 feet long and can interfere even with massless attacks, like magical blasts or gouts of flame. The screen imposes a –2 circumstance penalty to the attack rolls of spell effects even if they don't create physical objects. It also protects against area effects that pass through the screen, granting standard cover to creatures on the opposite side of the screen from the center or origin point of an area.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** Your screen persists for longer, and you can detonate it in an explosive counterattack. The duration of the spell increases to 1 minute. You can Dismiss the spell. If there are any projectiles trapped in the screen, you can cast [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) to fire them at one creature as part of Dismissing the spell. Measure the range for the telekinetic projectile from where the vector screen was, instead of from you.

</column>
name: Vector Screen

markdown:
**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 17 **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature --- You telekinetically animate a weapon that's unattended or on your person. It brandishes itself at a foe of your choice as if wielded by an invisible duelist. When you first Cast the Spell, the weapon automatically flies to the target and Strikes. It moves along with its target, always remaining within reach. Each time you Sustain the Spell, the weapon either Changes Partners or Strikes. The weapon's attacks use and contribute to your multiple attack penalty.
  • **Change Partners** Change the weapon's target to a different creature within 30 feet. The weapon flies to its new target.
  • **Strike** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The weapon attacks its target using your spell attack modifier. On a hit, the weapon deals 3d6 damage, of a type determined by the weapon (if the weapon has the [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) trait or can otherwise deal multiple types of damage, you choose each time you attack).
--- **Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6. --- **Amp** The weapon's attacks increase in strength, and your control is fine enough for advanced technique. The damage dice for the weapon's Strike change from d6s to d10s. When you Cast or Sustain the spell, you can choose from the following options in addition to the standard ones.
  • **Guard** Rather than attacking, the weapon grants a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against melee attacks to the creature it's following. The bonus lasts until the start of your next turn or until the weapon Changes Partners, whichever comes first.
  • **Push** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The weapon attempts to [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) its target, using your spell attack modifier instead of an Athletics check to determine the results of the Push.
name: Dancing Blade

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 3" pfs="Standard">
[Omnidirectional Scan](/Spells.aspx?ID=2551)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=689" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 18

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 30-foot emenation

**Target** 1 ally and 1 enemy
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the start of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

You quickly view your surroundings from a variety of angles, your senses constructing an overall mental picture. You can choose to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) the entire emanation of the spell.

 Regardless of whether you Seek, target one ally and one enemy in the area; if a target is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from you, you automatically succeed at the flat check to target it with this spell. You prepare to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) the target ally on an attack roll against the target enemy. If you take this Aid reaction, you use your spell attack modifier and proficiency rank on your check to Aid. If you critically fail the roll to Aid, you get a failure instead.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** Your scans are so minute that you can project your foe's likely strengths and weaknesses. You learn which of the target enemy's saving throws is highest and which is lowest, and you and all allies within 30 feet gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attacks and damage against the creature.

 If you take the Aid reaction you prepared for and succeed at your roll, these bonuses increase to +2 on the attack you're Aiding. On a critical success, they increase to +3, or to +4 if you have legendary proficiency in spell attack modifier.

</column>
name: Omnidirectional Scan

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 18 **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 ally and 1 enemy **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You see just a split second into the future and glean how an enemy will move, making it easier for your allies to strike it mid-action. Make a Perception check against the target enemy's Will DC or Deception DC, whichever is higher. The GM may judge a different DC is more appropriate if it's higher; for instance, using the Warfare Lore DC of a military captain. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target ally gains a reaction that lets it make a melee Strike against the target enemy, triggered if the enemy leaves a square within the ally’s reach. If the ally already has an ability that lets it make melee Strikes as a reaction, such as Reactive Strike, the ally can forgo gaining the temporary reaction to instead gain a +2 statusbonus to melee Strikes it makes as reactions.
**Success** As critical success, but the status bonus an ally gets if it forgoes the reaction is +1.
**Failure** The spell has no effect. --- **Amp** The spell has the same result on a failure as on a success (but still no effect on a critical failure). Each time you Sustain the Spell, you can choose an additional ally to target, granting them the same benefit as the initial target ally.
name: Foresee the Path

<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />

<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 20

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You deal cold or fire damage to an enemy.

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You stockpile thermal energy in a magical wheel-like construct that lets you burn opponents with cold or freeze their bodies with heat. When you Cast the Spell, the wheel has one mote of thermal energy, and when you use a cold or fire effect or deal cold or fire damage, the wheel spins, siphoning off a bit of energy and gaining another mote. The wheel can't gain motes more than once on a given turn, and the maximum number of motes is equal to _entropic wheel's_rank.

 When you Cast a Spell that deals fire damage, the target also takes cold damage equal to the number of motes in the _entropic wheel_. When you Cast a Spell that deals cold damage, the target also takes fire damage equal to the number of motes in the _entropic wheel_. This applies only to the initial damage of the spell, not to any persistent damage or ongoing effects.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** You gain two motes instead of one when you Cast the Spell and each time the wheel gains another mote.

</column>
name: Entropic Wheel

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 5" pfs="Standard">
[Redistribute Potential](/Spells.aspx?ID=2555)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 20

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** 2 adjacent 5-foot squares

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude

</row>

</column>

---

Energy attempts to balance out, but with your magic, you can shunt all the energy in one area to another. Creatures in either area take 4d4 damage with a basic Fortitude save. Choose one of the squares to steal heat, dealing cold damage, and the other to concentrate the stolen heat, dealing fire damage. A creature that fails its save also becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 from numbness if it's in the area of stolen heat or [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 from heat stroke if it's in the area of concentrated heat; these conditions last until the start of your next turn. If a creature is large enough to be in both squares, you choose only one of the areas for it to attempt its save against; it's unaffected by the other area.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** ou can affect the flow of energy on a much grander scale. Instead of two 5-foot squares, you create two 10-foot bursts. They still must be adjacent to one another and can overlap. A creature in both areas can still be affected by only one of your choice. The spell’s damage increases to 6d6. The conditions last for 1 minute.

**Amp Heightened (+1)**
Increase the damage by 2d6 instead of 1d4.
</column>
name: Redistribute Potential

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Forbidden Thought](/Spells.aspx?ID=2556)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 21

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Will

</row>

</column>

---

You place a psychic lock in a foe's mind that prevents it from a specific action. Choose “Strike,” “Stride,” “Cast a Spell,” or a specific action you know the creature to have (such as a [dragon's](/Traits.aspx?ID=581) magic breath). If the creature attempts that action on its next turn, it must surmount your lock to do so, causing it to take 2d6 mental damage (with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). The target is then temporarily immune for 1 minute.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** Your telepathic push is particularly shocking to those who resist it. If the target fails its save, it's also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.

</column>
name: Forbidden Thought

markdown:
**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 21 **Range** 30 feet (see text) **Target** 1 or 2 creatures other than yourself (see text) --- Nothing is so contagious as a thought. You place either a pleasant thought or a terrifying one in a creature's mind. You can then plant the same thought in a second creature's mind. You can't choose a creature that's already been a target of this casting of contagious idea, nor can you choose yourself. The second target can be beyond the range of the spell, but it must be within 30 feet of the first target.
  • Pleasant Thought The target is soothed by a pleasant memory, gaining 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute.
  • Terrifying Thought You plant a nameless unease in the target's mind. The target must attempt a Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 and is then temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Failure** The target becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target becomes frightened 3. --- **Heightened (+1)** The temporary Hit Points for a pleasant thought increase by 1. --- **Amp** You can choose to spread the thought from the second target to a third target, in the same manner as spreading it from the first target to the second. A pleasant thought grants twice as many temporary Hit Points (10, plus an additional 2 for each rank heightened).
name: Contagious Idea

[Imaginary Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=2559)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=540" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=610" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC

</row>

</column>

---

You create a simple weapon of force and lash out, its arc following your hand. Make a melee spell attack roll against your target's AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 force damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage and can choose to detonate your weapon to push the target 10 feet away from you.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** You form multiple force weapons to lash out at multiple foes. You can target two creatures, making an attack against each. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls.

**Amp Heightened (+1)**
The damage increases by 2d6 instead of 1d6.
</column>
name: Imaginary Weapon

[Astral Rain](/Spells.aspx?ID=2560)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=610" />

**Range** 60 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

You fling a mass of furious thoughts into the air, where they expand into numerous blades or other shapes that rain down. Any creature that's in the area or enters the area before the start of your next turn takes 4d4 bludgeoning or piercing damage (your choice), with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex saving throw.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** The area changes to a 5-foot burst, and you can Sustain the Spell up to 1 minute. The first time each round that you Sustain the Spell, you can move the burst up to 20 feet.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 5" pfs="Standard">
[Hologram Cage](/Spells.aspx?ID=2561)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** 1 cube 20 feet on each side

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You weave light into a cube of vivid and fantastic patterns. The walls shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2401) for 10 feet (and dim light for the next 20 feet) and completely block line of sight; creatures within the cage can't be seen by creatures outside of it, though they can see each other, and creatures outside of it can't see into it. You can Dismiss the spell, and if you Cast the Spell again, your previous _hologram cage_ ends.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The range of the spell increases to 80 feet, and you can expand the cube to be 25 feet on each side.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** Force walls make the cage's barrier physical as well as visual. Each 10-foot-by-10-foot section of the cage's wall has AC 10, Hardness equal to double the spell's rank, and HP equal to quadruple the spell's rank. The spell is disrupted if any of its walls would pass through a creature when you cast it.

</column>
name: Hologram Cage

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Distortion Lens](/Spells.aspx?ID=2562)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 23

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 5-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a magical lens that distorts space as best suits you. You create the lens in an area in range, even suspended in midair. If your or an ally's ranged attack passes through the lens, the attack gains an additional 10 feet of range; if an enemy's ranged attack would pass through the lens, it requires an additional 10 feet of range to move through, though the enemy knows before using its ability whether the interference puts the target out of range. An ally whose space overlaps the lens can increase the range of its ranged attacks, but an enemy whose space overlaps the lens doesn't reduce the range of its ranged attacks.

 The first time each round you Sustain the Spell, you can choose to relocate it to another area within range. The lens disappears if you cast _distortion lens_ again.

---
**Heightened (+3)** The lens increases or decreases the range of abilities by an additional 5 feet.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** You can lock the lens in place, allowing it to affect creatures more strongly. The duration of the spell becomes 1 minute, but you cannot Sustain the spell to reposition the lens. Once during a Medium or smaller ally's move action, the ally can move into and out of the lens's area without those spaces counting against the total distance moved. Conversely, the lens is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for your enemies.

</column>
name: Distortion Lens

[Ghostly Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=2563)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />

</row>

---

You gain resistance 2 to all damage (except force).

---
**Heightened (+2)** The resistance increases by 1.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** Your insubstantiality is complete enough that you can pass through other creatures, causing painful interference effects when you do so. Once each round when you Stride, [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), or [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), you can pass through one creature's space during that action without needing to attempt a check to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), though you can't end your turn in its space. When you pass through a creature's space in this way, you deal 2d6 force damage to that creature, with a basic Fortitude save.

**Amp Heightened (+2)**
Increase the damage dealt by phasing through a creature by 1d6.
</column>
name: Ghostly Shift

<summary>
Your body becomes insubstantial as you partially phase out.
</summary>
summary_markdown: Your body becomes insubstantial as you partially phase out.

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 5" pfs="Standard">
[Tesseract Tunnel](/Spells.aspx?ID=2564)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Psychic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=894" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=710" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 23

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You race from point to point, tearing open a tunnel in space. You create a portal in your current space and then Stride, creating another portal in the space you end your Stride. Until the beginning of your next turn, any creature that enters the first portal can immediately transport itself to the exit portal as part of its move action, which adds the teleportation trait to its movement.

 You can have only one tesseract tunnel open at a time; creating another causes the first to immediately close. You can use tesseract tunnel while [Burrowing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.

---

<column gap="tiny">
**Amp** Physical movement is no longer necessary to connect two points in space. When you cast _tesseract tunnel_, you simply create a tunnel that ends in a square within a range equal to your Speed; you can choose to immediately teleport to the space at the far end of the tunnel. Furthermore, the tunnel can be traversed in both directions, instead of only from entrance to exit. Finally, the spell's duration changes to 1 minute. You can Dismiss the spell.

</column>
name: Tesseract Tunnel

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[False Nature](/Spells.aspx?ID=2565)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 93

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deity**
[Nyuo-Ogh](/Deities.aspx?ID=584)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** one unattended item or one item you’re holding
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until your next daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You create an illusion that makes an object seem to be something entirely different. When you Cast this Spell, speak aloud a declarative statement that the target is a different, nonspecific object. Your statement can't presuppose any other facts. For instance, you could claim an object is “a stone” or “an antidote,” but not “the Starstone” or “the antidote to the lethal poison you already drank.” Creatures who previously observed the target receive an immediate Will save to disbelieve, but on a failure, the illusion temporarily rewrites their memory that the object was something different. A creature who failed their Will save, or didn't already see the item's original form, must Interact with the illusion as normal to receive a Perception check against the spell DC to disbelieve. When the spell ends, targets remember the object's true nature, even if they never disbelieved the spell.
name: False Nature
